pages.text: # 3. Team Management Team Management Team Transfer: You may transfer a team from the previous season without any restrictions. Selling Players: Players can be sold after a match for half their price. Roster Constraints: You cannot sell or remove a player if doing so would leave the team with fewer than 7 players. Contracts Contract Terms: Upon team formation, the coach signs a contract with the team. The contract renewal time is announced by the Commissioner and typically occurs at the end of the season. Team Buyout: To purchase the team from the league treasury, 600,000 gold will be allocated, plus the funds in the team's treasury at the time of renewal, up to a total maximum of 900,000 gold. Agent Fees: A player who has completed a contract will incur an additional 20,000 gold in agent fees for each contract completed after the first. For example, after 3 successful contracts, a player will cost 40,000 more to buy out. This increase is not counted toward the total team value. Player Departure: Players who do not sign a contract leave the team. Retention: After signing a new contract, a player retains all experience, acquired skills, and injuries. Dissolution: You may dissolve your team and choose not to renew the contract if you wish. Contract Renewal Health Recovery: Healing Niggling Injuries: Roll a d6 for each Niggling Injury; add a +1 modifier if you have an Apothecary. On a 4+, the vacation and rest work wonders, and the injury is successfully healed. If you fail, the injury remains with the player. Healing Serious Injuries: Roll a d6 for each stat reduction on the player; add a +1 modifier if you have an Apothecary. On a 4+, the treatment is successful, the characteristic is restored to its previous value, and the player receives a Niggling Injury. If you fail, the injury remains with the player. Removing Hatred: Roll a d6 for each acquired Hatred skill on the player. On a 4+, the player forgets their old grudges, and the skill is removed from the player's profile. Staff & Personnel: Assistant coaches, cheerleaders, and apothecaries go on unpaid leave. Rerolls are lost. Select the players you wish to keep on the team and pay their cost, plus any applicable agent fees. Remove the remaining players. Count the number of upgrades the departing players had and roll that many d6s; for every 1-2 rolled, you lose one Fan Factor. Purchase new players, staff, and rerolls as needed. Season Structure and Scoring Match Conduct: Rounds: One round lasts two weeks, starting on a Monday and ending on a Sunday. Deadlines: Each match must be played within its designated round. Locations: Games can be played at the Litch Club, at Ded Max's Painting Evenings, or at someone's home by prior arrangement. Virtual Play: If it is impossible to play in real life, the match can be played using the Tabletop Simulator (TTS) mod. Scheduling: Time proposals for matches must be submitted by the end of Friday. If a coach only provides scheduling proposals over the weekend and the match is ultimately not played, the Commissioner will favor the coach who provided their availability in advance. Incomplete Matches: If a match is not played within the round, the result will be determined by the Commissioner. Season Start: At the beginning of the season, all missed game penalties are removed from all players. League Points: 3 points for a win. 1 point for a draw. 2 points for a technical win. 0 points for a loss. +1 point if the game ends with a margin of 3+ touchdowns. +1 point if you conceded 0 touchdowns (you must score at least one yourself). +2 points if the opponent could not field a team for the match (e.g., due to too many injuries). End-of-Season Prizes: At the end of the season, all teams not in the top rankings receive winnings: 10k for each match played. 20k for each victory. 10k for each draw. Rookie Protection: For the first 3 games after a team's creation, all injuries inflicted on its players are counted as Badly Hurt. Technical Win & Tournament Structure Technical Win: The winning team is awarded 2 MVP rolls and (d3+2) 10k gold. Tournament Bracket: Matches are assigned using the Swiss system. Tie-breakers: The Buchholz coefficient and head-to-head results are used for tie-breakers, followed by the total number of touchdowns and injuries. Playoff Qualification: Depending on the number of participants, the players at the top of the standings will receive an automatic bye into the tournament's playoff winner's bracket Additional Rules Special Rules and Gameplay Changes Prohibited Inducements: Special Hired Wizards, Biased Referees, and Famous Coaching Staff are not used in the league. DOCX Rules Adjustments: Some rules from the original Sevens and Blood Bowl have been modified for more enjoyable and deliberate gameplay. Most player costs have been recalculated; these changes can be viewed in the team table. DOCX+ 1 General Gameplay Rules MVP Selection: Choose 3 players and roll a d3 to decide which one receives 5 SPP. DOCX Kick-off: When the ball scatters on kick-off, roll 1d6 and halve the result (rounding down); the result can be zero. DOCX Team Rerolls: The cost of Team Rerolls is not doubled during team drafting, but it is doubled if purchased during the season. DOCX SPP for Knock Outs: A player who knocks out an opponent receives 1 SPP. DOCX Experience (SPP): Actions similar to Block or their consequences grant SPP according to current rules (1 for KO, 2 for Injury). For example, Stab, Chainsaw, Bombardier, and pushing a player off the pitch can now grant experience. DOCX+ 1 A Foul action grants experience just like a Block. DOCX A Throw Teammate action grants the thrower experience for inflicting Injuries and KOs, just like a Block. DOCX Inducement Tiering: When determining inducement budgets, the team's tier is considered. Tier 2 teams receive 200k when playing against Tier 1; Tier 3 teams receive 300k against Premier League teams or 100k against Tier 2 teams. DOCX+ 1 Injury Table: Use the injury table from the "big format" (standard) Blood Bowl. DOCX Setup: The team setup is moved 3 squares forward. Sevens now has a Line of Scrimmage. You must place at least 3 players on the Line of Scrimmage in the central segment. No more than 1 player is allowed in each side segment. DOCX+ 3 Touchdown Restrictions: A touchdown cannot be scored on the first turn of any drive. If your player is in the opponent's end zone at the end of your first turn, you do not score, but if they are still there at the start of your second turn, the drive ends immediately and you score a touchdown. Exception: A touchdown can be scored on the first turn of a drive if the player was thrown via Throw Teammate. DOCX+ 2 Leaping: Jumping over prone players is prohibited unless the player has the Leap skill. DOCX Mega-Stars: Star Players with the Mega-Star tag occupy both available Star Player slots. DOCX Bribes: Bribes trigger automatically. DOCX Secure the Ball!: The "no ball pickup" restriction radius is reduced to 1 square. DOCX Bribery and Corruption: Allows purchasing up to 3 bribes per team for 50k each, which increase Team Value (TV). DOCX TV Exceptions: Cheerleaders and assistant coaches do not count toward Team Value. DOCX Painting Bonus: A fully painted team receives an additional 30k after a match. DOCX Throw-Ins: Throw-ins are resolved using 1d6. Team Changes Necromantic Horror, Shambling Undead, and Tomb Kings: Gained access to hire a Mortuary Assistant (allows a regeneration reroll once per game) for 50k gold. Nurgle: Gained access to hire a Plague Doctor (allows a regeneration reroll once per game, or acts as an apothecary) for 50k gold. Team Captain: All teams now have the Team Captain special rule. Ogre: May designate an Ogre Runt Punter player as captain. Brawling Brutes: Some teams have gained the Brawling Brutes rule; refer to the team table for details. Passing Virtuosos: Some teams have gained the new Passing Virtuosos special rule. Teams with this rule receive 2 SPP for touchdowns, and a successful catch during a pass grants the receiver 1 SPP upon completion. Slann: Now a Tier 1 team. All Slann players replace the Pogo skill with Leap. Halfling: Gained access to the Old World Classic league. Goblin: Pogoer, Ooligan, and Doom Diver players now have the Lineman tag. Chaos Chosen: Chaos Blocker players now have Iron Hard Skin. Norse: The Beer Boar player now has the Lineman tag; the Norse Raider Lineman no longer has the Unsteady rule. High Elf: Updated according to current rules, and costs have been revised. Elven Union: Elf Lineman players have lost the Fumblerooskie skill. Gnome: The team list has been completely reworked. Orc: The Big Un no longer has the Mighty Blow skill. Black Orc: Black Orc movement (MV) increased to 5, and their cost is now 110k. The team has been moved to Tier 1. Chaos Dwarf: The team has been reworked. Dwarf: Dwarf Blocker Lineman now have 'S' as primary access and 'D' as secondary access. The Deathroller now costs 180k, and the team has been moved to Tier 1. Khorne: The Bloodborn Marauder Lineman has had their PA reduced to 6+, AV increased to 9+, gained the Brawler skill, and now costs 60k. The team has been moved to Tier 1. Tomb Kings: Ancient Skeleton Lineman players now have primary access to 'D'. Blitz-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 65k. Thro-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 85k. The team has been moved to Tier 1, and the Brawling Brutes tag has been removed. Kislev: The team has been reworked. Vampire: Thrall Lineman now cost 40k. Vampire Thrower now cost 115k. It is now forbidden to hire an apothecary for the team. : Skill Changes Claws, Mighty Blow, and Piledriver: These skills now fully stack (with Claws and Mighty Blow, you break any armor on a 7+). Piledriver: Completely changed. After an armor roll from a Block action, the player using the skill can become Placed Prone to roll again. This does not work with skills that replace Block and has been moved to the Strength category. Hypnotic Gaze: Partially changed; can now only be used once per turn. Brawler: Completely changed; allows for a one-time reroll of a single die during a Block. Cannot be used in negative dice blocks. Plague Ridden/Masters of Undeath: Completely changed; when a player with this rule kills an opponent, their coach gains a Lineman of their own team (Shambling Undead can choose between a Skeleton or a Zombie). Throw Teammate: Now a passive skill. Blood Lust: New wording. Roll a d6 after declaring any action: on a 2+, the turn continues normally. On a 1, the player must "feed." The action is lost; the player may choose an adjacent teammate (without the Vampire or Undead tags) to perform an injury roll against, treating all results as Badly Hurt. This roll causes a turnover only if the Thrall was holding the ball. Movement may be declared before the bite; after the bite, the player's activation ends. If no bite is performed, a turnover occurs. Accurate and Cannoneer: Merged into one skill (Accurate), which provides +1 to all passes. Dodge: Completely changed; now only allows a reroll for dodge tests. Evasive (New): The player can treat a result of 5 as 3-4 when being blocked, and Stumble results as Push Back. Tackle: Completely changed; it now ignores the Evasive skill. Arm Bar: Completely changed; prevents rerolls on dodge tests for players in the owner's tackle zone. Grants SPP like Block if the opponent is injured or knocked out while dodging away. Block: Partially changed; only works during the player's own activation. Thick Skull: Moved to the General category. Sneaky Git: You are no longer removed on doubles when fouling during an armor break. Stab: No longer ends movement during a Blitz. Shiv (New): Works like Stab but can only be used once per turn among all players with the skill. Decay: Completely changed; when receiving an injury, roll two dice and choose the highest. Safe Pass: Protects against any Fumble, not just on a natural 1. Fumblerooski: Moved to the Agility category. Pogo: Can only be used once per activation. Secret Weapon: Only works if the player was fielded for the drive. Shadowing: Now a General skill. Frenzy: Now optional; must be declared immediately before the first block action of the activation. Monstrous Mouth: Limits holding to 1 player (unless used with Multiple Block, then up to 2). Big Hand: Additionally allows picking up the ball from an adjacent square (but not while in an opponent's control zone). Disturbing Presence: Works on ball pickup (still does not affect Secure the Ball); does not work if the player is prone. Foul Appearance: Now triggers on a 1-2 roll and does not end the player's activation. Iron Hard Skin: Now grants +1 AV in addition to basic properties; the modifier ignore only works during a block. Tentacles: Now works on a raw 4+ roll, regardless of the models' strength. Bone Hook (New Mutation): Before a Foul action, you may move the target player 1 square to an adjacent cell (happens before assist calculation). Leap: Now always works on an unmodifiable 4+ roll (improved by the Very Long Legs mutation). Elvenball (New General Skill): Grants the ability to move after a Secure the Ball action. Guard: Now only works during the opponent's turn. Offensive (New General Skill): Grants an unblockable assist for a block during your turn. Defensive: Now cancels the Offensive skill. Breathe Fire: Does not cause Knocked Down on a roll of 1. Diving Catch / On the Ball: Work when Punt is declared. Jump Up: Allows replacing Block with special actions (e.g., Stab) and declaring active skills while Prone. Multiple Block: Works on special actions that replace Block. Juggernaut: Also cancels Foul Appearance. Kick: Changed; instead of a d3 roll, halve the d6 value during kickoff (rounding down, result can be 0). Leader: Triggers at the start of a drive. Changes to "Favoured Of..." Real Bonuses: The Favoured of rule now provides actual bonuses. Deity Selection: If a team has a choice for this rule, you must select one of the following options during team registration: Undivided, Khorne, Nurgle, Tzeentch, Slaanesh, or Hashut. Skill Acquisition: Every time a player receives an advancement, roll a d8. Undivided triggers on 1. Slaanesh triggers on 6. Nurgle triggers on 7. Khorne triggers on 8. Tzeentch triggers on 9 (or 6). Hashut triggers on 4. Additional Skill: A player may receive one of the skills corresponding to your choice in addition to the one taken for a regular advancement. This skill increases the player's value, though it does not increase the player's rank. Random Selection: If a player has 'M' access in secondary skills or no access at all, they must choose the skill randomly. Skill Lists: Undivided: Prehensile Tail, Extra Arm, Disturbing Presence. Tzeentch: Two Heads, Extra Arm, Very Long Legs. Khorne: Horns, Iron Hard Skin, Prehensile Tail. Slaanesh: Tentacles, Foul Appearance, Extra Arm. Nurgle: Tentacles, Monstrous Mouth, Bone Hook. Hashut: Iron Hard Skin, Horns, Bone Hook. Coach's Safe Deposits: After each game, you may send up to 50k in winnings to a safe for a "rainy day," as well as any number of league winnings. Restrictions: These funds cannot be spent until the end of the season. Usage: After a redraft, you may add up to 300k from these reserves when building any team. Player Advancement (Leveling Up) Players accumulate SPP and spend them to acquire skills in accordance with the base game rules. The player's value increases according to the following rules: Primary skill: +20k Secondary skill: +40k AV: +10k PA: +20k MA: +30k AG: +40k ST: +50k Elite Combos Cost Increase: Elite skill combinations increase a player's value by an additional 15k if they were acquired during an advancement. Elite Skills: The following skills are considered elite: Claws + Mighty Blow Guard + Defensive Wrestle + Evasive No Rank Primary Secondary Stat 1 Experienced 6 SPP 10 SPP 14 SPP 2 Veteran 8 SPP 12 SPP 16 SPP 3 Emerging Star 12 SPP 16 SPP 20 SPP 4 Star 16 SPP 20 SPP 24 SPP 5 Superstar 20 SPP 24 SPP 28 SPP 6 Legend 30 SPP 34 SPP 38 SPP d8 Stat 1 AV +1 2 AV/PA +1 3-4 AV/MA/PA +1 5 MA/PA +1 6 AG/MA +1 7 AG/ST +1 8 +1 Skill Table Agility Devious Mutation Passing Strength 1 Catch Dirty Player Block Big Hand Accurate Arm Bar 2 Diving Catch Eye Gouge Dauntless Claws Cloud Burster Brawler 3 Diving Tackle Lethal Flight Fend Disturbing Presence Dump-off Break Tackle 3 Dodge Lone Fouler Frenzy Extra Arms Give and Go Bullseye 4 Defensive Put the Boot In Kick Foul Appearance Hail Mary Pass Grab 5 Hit and Run Quick Foul Pro Horns Leader Guard 6 Jump Up Saboteur Steady Footing Iron Hard Skin Nerves of Steel Juggernaut 7 Leap Sneaky Git Strip Ball Monstrous Mouth On the Ball Mighty Blow 8 Safe Pair of Hands Shiv Sure Hands Prehensile Tail Pass Multiple Block 9 Sidestep Tackle Tentacles Punt Stand Firm 10 Sprint Taunt Two Heads Safe Pass Strong Arm 11 Sure Feet Wrestle Very Long Legs Pile Driver 12 Evasive Thick Skull Bone Hook 13 Fumblerooski Shadowing 14 Offensive 15 Elvenball Inducements Inducements The following inducements are available in the league: 0-5 Cheerleaders (10k): Receive +1 cheerleader per game. 0-5 Assistant Coaches (10k): Receive +1 assistant coach per game. 0-3 Nuffle's Prayers (15k): Roll on the Nuffle's Prayers table; you may reroll results you already have. 0-1 Team Mascot (25k): Once per half, grants an additional Team Reroll. When used, roll a d6: on a 4+, the reroll works; on a 1-3, it is burned, but you can immediately use a regular reroll instead. Also grants a reroll of 1 for the Cheering Fans event. 0-1 Weather Mage (20k): Once per game, at the start of your turn, you may roll on the weather table, adding a modifier of -2 to +2 to the result after rolling. 0-2 Bloodweiser Keg (or Blitzer's Best) (40k): Until the end of the game, your rolls to recover from Knock Out receive +1. 0-3 Bribe (100k, or 50k for Bribery and Corruption): When your player is sent off, you may spend a bribe to cancel this effect. The turnover is also canceled. 0-6 Additional Training (85k): Gain a Team Reroll until the end of the game. 0-1 Mortuary Assistant (100k, only for Masters of Undeath): Once per game, you may reroll a regeneration check. 0-1 Plague Doctor (100k, only for Favoured of Nurgle): Once per game, you may reroll a regeneration check or apply an apothecary effect. 0-2 Wandering Apothecary (100k, only for teams capable of hiring an Apothecary): Grants an additional use of an apothecary. 0-1 Biased Referee (100k, or 60k for Bribery and Corruption): After your opponent performs a Foul, roll a d6: on a 5+, the fouling player is sent off. You receive +1 to rolls when disputing a referee's decision. 0-1 Hired Wizard (150k): Once per game, after any player's turn, you may cast one of the following spells: Fireball: Choose a square; roll a d6 for each player in that and adjacent squares. On a 4+, the player falls; make an armor roll with a +1 modifier. Toad-Toad: Choose a player and roll a d6. If the result equals or exceeds the player's Strength (6 is always a success), turn them into a toad until the end of the drive. Any injury rolls against the toad are considered Badly Hurt; an apothecary cannot be used. Toad stats: MV 5, ST 1, AG 2+, PA -, AV 5+ (Pogo, Evasive, Dodge, Stunty, No Ball, Titchy, Very Long Legs). 0-1 Rowdy Rookies (100k, only for Low cost Linemen): Gain 2d3+1 journeymen, ignoring the team size limit. 0-1 Halfling Master Chef (300k, or 100k for Halfling): At the start of each half, roll a d6 for each of the opponent's Team Rerolls; on a 4+, the reroll transfers to your team. 0-2 Star Players (0-4 for Stunty Leagues): Hire a Star Player from your league, ignoring team limits. 0-1 Vishnevsky Ointment (60k): Once per game, if a player leaves the game due to injury, you may return them to the reserves. However, at the end of the game, apply all effects they should have received from that injury. 0-1 WAAAGH! Drummer (50k, only for Badlands Brawl): At the start of each drive, after kickoff, roll 4d6; for each roll of 4+, move any player 1 square toward your opponent's end zone. You may choose the same player multiple times. 0-3 Frolicking Nurgling (15k, only for Favoured of Nurgle): +1 cheerleader and assistant coach. You may exceed the maximum. 0-1 Rune Priest (50k, only for Old World Classic and Worlds Edge Superleague): At the start of each drive, before the kick, choose one player without the Loner trait and roll a d6: on a 4+, select a rune and apply it to the player (cannot use this effect again for the game); on 2-3, the rune fails but you can repeat in the next drive; on 1, the rune fails and the effect is locked for the game. Rune of Might: Player gains Mighty Blow until the end of the drive. Rune of Fury: Player gains Dauntless and Frenzy until the end of the drive. Rune of Speed: Player gains Sure Feet and Sprint until the end of the drive. Rune of Iron: Player gains +1 AV and Stand Firm until the end of the drive. Rune of Force: Player gains Horns and Juggernaut until the end of the drive. 0-1 Ballistics Expert (40k, or 30k for Halfling): Each drive, during kickoff, before the ball scatters, roll a d3: 1: Nothing happens. 2: You may reduce the scatter distance by 1. 3: You may apply a modifier of -2 to +2 to the d6 and d8 values. 0-1 Halfling "Surprise" Pot (80k, or 60k for Halfling): Once per game, after the opponent's turn, choose a square and roll a d6: 6: Pot hits the target. 2-5: Scatter the target square 3 times. 1: d3 players in your reserves move to Knock Out. The player in the target square falls; make an armor roll with a +2 modifier. Then roll a d6 for each player in adjacent squares; on a 4+, they become Placed Prone. 0-3 Bottles of "Grape Day" (30k, available to Stunty teams): At the start of each drive, before kickoff, choose d3 players with the Stunty rule; until the end of the drive, they gain Dauntless, Frenzy, and Really Stupid (4+). 0-1 Mark of Chaos (50k, only for Favoured of...): At the start of the match, the chosen player gains skills until the end of the game. Does not work on Star Players. Skills depend on your choice of Favoured of...: Undivided: Tentacles, Horns. Khorne: Piledriver, Jump Up. Nurgle: Disturbing Presence, Foul Appearance. Tzeentch: Pro, Two Heads. Slaanesh: Dodge, Leap. Hashut: Iron Hard Skin, Claw. F.A.Q. Q: Can a Team Reroll be used more than once per activation? A: Yes. Q: What happens if a bomb is thrown at a player with the My Ball rule? A: The bomb explodes immediately in the receiving player's square (Commissioner's discretion). Q: What happens during an inaccurate bomb pass? A: It scatters 3 times and explodes in the final square. Q: Does a player receive SPP for a knockout if the Thick Skull rule was applied? A: Yes. Q: A player with AG 1+ dodges out of any number of tackle zones-do they always dodge on a 2+? Since after the roll, the test result is 1, but it's not a natural 1, does that mean the 1+ test is passed? A: No, by analogy with passing, any 1 after modifiers is a failure (Commissioner's discretion). Q: Can active skills that work at the start of an activation (e.g., Stab, Chainsaw) be used if the player starts the activation in a Prone state? A: Yes. We just want to play like it's 2015, so stand up and stab, foul, etc.. Q: How do Frenzy and Eye Gouge interact with Stand Firm? A: They trigger on any result involving a Push Back, even if the push is ignored. Q: How does Multiple Block work in conjunction with Stab, Projectile Vomit, and other actions that replace Block? A: Declare Multiple Block before any other skills; both Block actions will be replaced by the special action. Q: Can Bullseye and Strong Arm be taken by a player with Kick Teammate? A: Yes. Per the rulebook (RAW), this is forbidden, but it appears to be a wording error. What is Stalling? If, at the start of your team's turn, a player on your team has the ball and can score without making any dice rolls, then performing any actions other than "reliable" actions will be considered stalling. Additionally, during this turn, if the ball carrier declares any action other than Move or Blitz (provided conditions for reliability are met), it is considered stalling. Reliable Actions Movement: This must be done without GFI (Going For It), dodges, or any dice rolls that could potentially lead to a turnover. If the player holding the ball does not score during their activation, it is considered stalling. Block: Must be a 2 or 3-dice block with a team reroll available, and the active player must have the Block or Wrestle skill. In the event of a "double skull" result, you are obligated to use a team reroll. Intentionally rerolling using Brawler, Hatred, or Pro to force a "double skull" result, as well as choosing a "skull" or "both down" result without applying Block or Wrestle, is considered stalling. Throw Teammate: Must be performed by a player without the Loner trait, must not involve throwing the ball carrier, and must be performed only when a team reroll is available. Foul: Must be performed with a bribe available and no modifiers that could negate the bribe , . 6 ( 4-, ) . . , , . . , 6 . , . (1 1): . , "" - . , . 1-3 , " "... . 4-5 ? "" 10 . 6 "", . pages.title: 4. Match Procedures
pages.body:
# 4. Match Procedures

League Formats

Season: A major event lasting about 3-4 months, with a defined start, end, and fixed roster of participants. Match schedules and times are strictly designated.

Tournaments: Events played over 1-2 days, typically serving as season finales or fun cup events.

Off-season: Friendly matches that can be played at any time, provided the team is not currently participating in another event. This is a great way to develop your team before the season. You may register up to 6 games per month in this format.

Match results must be reported within 1-3 days of completion. Failure to comply with this rule may result in penalties, up to and including the annulment of match results.

A single coach may register up to 4 teams in the league.

If either player wishes, the game can be played with a clock. The standard format is 45 minutes per person. The timer stops for both players during pre-game preparations, kick-offs, and similar downtime.

Match Procedures

Scheduling: For matches on designated game days, coaches must arrange the details with their opponents independently, using any convenient method.

Reporting: Match results should be reported to the commissioner using any convenient method.

Photos: It would be even better if you took some photos.

Match Procedures

Scheduling: For matches on designated game days, coaches must arrange the details with their opponents independently, using any convenient method.

Locations: Matches can be played at the Litch Club, at home, or at Ded Max's Painting Evenings.

Reporting: Match results should be reported to the commissioner using any convenient method.

Photos: It would be even better if you took some photos.

pages.title: 5. Patch Notes
pages.body:
# 5. Patch Notes

Season 2 Patch Notes

General

Many minor unlisted changes to player costs; please check.

OTD (One-Turn Touchdown) is permitted when using the Throw Teammate skill.

Friendly match limit: 3 per month.

Maximum team size increased to 14.

Secure the Ball! range is now 1 square.

Added "Coach's Safe" rule: After each game, you may send up to 50k in winnings to a safe for a "rainy day," as well as any number of league winnings. These cannot be spent until the end of the season; however, after a redraft, you may add up to 300k from these reserves when building any team.

Bribery and Corruption allows purchasing up to 3 Bribes per team; they increase Team Value (TV).

The costs of many players with 8+ armor have been recalculated; please check your team list.

The Weather Change event now checks weather on 1d6. The weather table has been adapted accordingly.

Passing Virtuosos: No longer grants extra experience for passing. Instead, the receiver of a successful Accurate pass also receives 1 SPP.

Any external armor reduction sources (mages, stadiums, etc.) are applied after skill-based armor reduction and cannot reduce the final armor value below 7+.

Fully painted teams receive an additional 30k gold after a match.

All Team Rerolls now cost 120k gold; they can be bought for 60k gold during team drafts as usual.

Captain rule changed: It grants the Captain player Pro. You must field them if able, and while they are on the field, roll 5+ when using a Reroll; on success, it is not consumed.

Block now only works if its owner declares a Block (e.g., if you are being blocked, Block does not work). The cost of some players with Block has been revised.

Dodge is split into Dodge and Evasive. Its effects are divided: Dodge allows rerolling leaving Tackle Zones; Evasive converts Stumble to Push Back. Many players lost one of these two skills.

For the first 3 games after creation, all injuries inflicted on team players are considered Badly Hurt.

Favoured Of...: Now rolls on a d8. Slaanesh triggers on 6, Nurgle on 7, Khorne on 8, Tzeentch on 9(6), Hashut on 4.

Teams

Slann: Now a Tier 1 team. All Slann players replace Pogo with Leap.

Halfling: Gained access to the Old World Classic league.

Goblin: Pogoer, Ooligan, and Doom Diver players now have the Lineman tag.

Chaos Chosen: Chaos Blocker now has Iron Hard Skin. Team moved to Tier 1.

Norse: Beer Boar now has the Lineman tag. Norse Raider Lineman no longer has Unsteady.

Amazon: Team moved to Tier 1. Linewoman costs 50k.

High Elf: Updated according to current rules; costs revised.

Wood Elf: Wood Elf Lineman now costs 70k.

Elven Union: Elf Lineman now costs 65k and lost Fumblerooskie.

Gnome: Team list completely reworked. Team moved to Tier 3.

Orc: Big Un no longer has Mighty Blow and now costs 100k.

Black Orc: Black Orc Move (MV) increased to 5; now costs 110k. Team moved to Tier 1.

Shambling Undead and Necromantic: Zombie Lineman access to 'D' is now secondary.

Chaos Dwarf: Hobgoblin Lineman now costs 40k. Chaos Dwarf Flamesmith lost Brawler and now costs 80k. Chaos Dwarf Blocker costs 65k. Bull Centaur gained Leap and Horns; now costs 150k. Players with the Dwarf tag lost access to 'A' (short legs, excluding Bull Centaur, as they have legs from a different creature). Team moved to Tier 1.

Dwarf: Dwarf Blocker Lineman: 'S' is now primary access, 'D' is secondary. Deathroller now costs 180k. Team moved to Tier 1.

Khorne: Bloodborn Marauder Lineman: PA reduced to 6+, AV increased to 9+, gained Brawler, and now costs 60k. Team moved to Tier 1.

Old World Alliance: Old World Dwarf Lineman: 'S' is now primary access, 'D' is secondary. Team moved to Tier 1.

Tomb Kings: Ancient Skeleton Lineman now has primary access to 'D' (remembers old strategies). Blitz-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 65k. Thro-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 85k. Team moved to Tier 1. Brawling Brutes tag removed.

Bretonnia: Bretonnian Squire now costs 50k.

Kislev: Lineman lost Fend, AV increased to 9+, now costs 50k. Kicker lost Fend and gained Punt. Wings PA lowered to 4+, lost Catch, gained Diving Catch, now costs 80k. Werebear removed from the team. New player Trained Bear added. Team gained Passing Virtuosos tag. Team moved to Tier 1.

Vampire: Thrall Lineman now costs 40k. Vampire Thrower now costs 115k. Apothecaries are banned.

Skills

Armbar forbids any dodge rerolls.

Shadowing is now a General skill.

Frenzy is now optional; the skill is declared immediately before the first block action in an activation.

Throw Teammate is now a passive skill.

Monstrous Mouth: Allows holding no more than 1 player. Exception: if used with Multiple Block, then up to 2 can be held.

Big Hand: In addition to old wording, now allows picking up the ball while in an adjacent square (but not while in an opponent's control zone).

Disturbing Presence: Works on ball pickup (still does not affect Secure the Ball). Does not work when the player is prone.

Foul Appearance: Now triggers on a 1-2 and does not end the player's activation upon triggering.

Iron Hard Skin: Now grants +1 to AV in addition to basic properties.

Tentacles: Now simply works on a raw roll of 4+ regardless of model strength.

New Mutation - Bone Hook: Before performing a foul action, you may move the target player to 1 square adjacent to the active player (occurs before calculating assists).

Leap: Now always works on an unmodifiable 4+ roll (improved by Very Long Legs mutation).

New General Skill - Elvenball: Allows movement after a Secure the Ball action.

Guard: Now only works during the opponent's turn.

Offensive (New General Skill): Grants an unblockable assist for a block during your turn.

Defensive: Now cancels the Offensive skill (functionally unchanged).

Stab: Removed from the skill list; it is now a trait, as it was before.

Shiv (New Skill): Works like Stab, but can only be used once per turn among all players with Shiv.

Bloodlust: New wording. Immediately after declaring any action by a player with this rule, roll a d6: on a 2+, the turn continues normally. If a 1 is rolled, the player must go "have a snack." The action is lost, and the player may choose a teammate without the Vampire or Undead tags in an adjacent square to roll for injury against, treating any result on the injury table as Badly Hurt. This roll causes a Turnover only if the Thrall was holding the ball. Before the bite, the player may declare movement. After the bite, the player's activation ends. If the bite is not performed, a Turnover occurs.

Breathe Fire: No longer causes Knocked Down on a roll of 1.

Brawler: Cannot be used in negative dice blocks.

Elite Skills: Deleted. Instead, the concept of "Elite Combos" is added. Having one on a player adds +15k to their cost. The following combos are elite: Claws+Mighty Blow, Guard+Defensive, Wrestle+Evasive, Sneaky Git+Dirty Player.

Diving Catch and On the Ball now work when Punt is declared.

Kick: Changed. Instead of a d6 roll, halve the d6 value during kickoff, rounding down. The final value can be 0.

Leader: Triggers at the start of a drive.

Inducements

Nuffle's Prayers now cost 15k.

Biased Referee now matches the rulebook wording. Instead of rerolling 1s, it grants +1 to disputing rolls.

Frolicking Nurgling now grants +1 Cheerleader and Assistant Coach until the end of the match; costs 15k.

Added new inducement: Giant.

Morg 'n' Thorg has been reworked; check the Star Player table; he is now truly powerful.

Bloodweiser Keg now costs 40k.

Cheerleaders now cost 10k (bear with it; it's justified by upcoming season rules).

Weather Mage now costs 20k.

Additional Training now costs 85k.

Free Agent removed from the available inducements list.

WAAAGH! Drummer now rolls 4d6 and allows moving your own players; chain pushes are forbidden.

Grape Day bottles now cost 30k.

Mark of Chaos - Khorne: Now grants Piledriver and Jump Up.

Favoured Of...

Addition to the second paragraph: If a player has 'M' access in secondary skills or none at all, they must choose a skill randomly.

Now rolls on a d8. Slaanesh triggers on 6, Nurgle on 7, Khorne on 8, Tzeentch on 9(6), Hashut on 4.

Undivided: Skill list replaced with: Prehensile Tail, Extra Arm, Disturbing Presence.

Tzeentch: Skill list replaced with: Two Heads, Extra Arm, Very Long Legs.

Khorne: Skill list replaced with: Horns, Iron Hard Skin, Prehensile Tail.

Slaanesh: Skill list replaced with: Tentacles, Foul Appearance, Extra Arm.

Nurgle: Skill list replaced with: Tentacles, Monstrous Mouth, Bone Hook.

Hashut: Skill list replaced with: Iron Hard Skin, Horns, Bone Hook.
pages.html:
5. Patch Notes

Season 2 Patch Notes

General

Many minor unlisted changes to player costs; please check.

OTD (One-Turn Touchdown) is permitted when using the Throw Teammate skill.

Friendly match limit: 3 per month.

Maximum team size increased to 14.

Secure the Ball! range is now 1 square.

Added "Coach's Safe" rule: After each game, you may send up to 50k in winnings to a safe for a "rainy day," as well as any number of league winnings. These cannot be spent until the end of the season; however, after a redraft, you may add up to 300k from these reserves when building any team.

Bribery and Corruption allows purchasing up to 3 Bribes per team; they increase Team Value (TV).

The costs of many players with 8+ armor have been recalculated; please check your team list.

The Weather Change event now checks weather on 1d6. The weather table has been adapted accordingly.

Passing Virtuosos: No longer grants extra experience for passing. Instead, the receiver of a successful Accurate pass also receives 1 SPP.

Any external armor reduction sources (mages, stadiums, etc.) are applied after skill-based armor reduction and cannot reduce the final armor value below 7+.

Fully painted teams receive an additional 30k gold after a match.

All Team Rerolls now cost 120k gold; they can be bought for 60k gold during team drafts as usual.

Captain rule changed: It grants the Captain player Pro. You must field them if able, and while they are on the field, roll 5+ when using a Reroll; on success, it is not consumed.

Block now only works if its owner declares a Block (e.g., if you are being blocked, Block does not work). The cost of some players with Block has been revised.

Dodge is split into Dodge and Evasive. Its effects are divided: Dodge allows rerolling leaving Tackle Zones; Evasive converts Stumble to Push Back. Many players lost one of these two skills.

For the first 3 games after creation, all injuries inflicted on team players are considered Badly Hurt.

Favoured Of...: Now rolls on a d8. Slaanesh triggers on 6, Nurgle on 7, Khorne on 8, Tzeentch on 9(6), Hashut on 4.

Teams

Slann: Now a Tier 1 team. All Slann players replace Pogo with Leap.

Halfling: Gained access to the Old World Classic league.

Goblin: Pogoer, Ooligan, and Doom Diver players now have the Lineman tag.

Chaos Chosen: Chaos Blocker now has Iron Hard Skin. Team moved to Tier 1.

Norse: Beer Boar now has the Lineman tag. Norse Raider Lineman no longer has Unsteady.

Amazon: Team moved to Tier 1. Linewoman costs 50k.

High Elf: Updated according to current rules; costs revised.

Wood Elf: Wood Elf Lineman now costs 70k.

Elven Union: Elf Lineman now costs 65k and lost Fumblerooskie.

Gnome: Team list completely reworked. Team moved to Tier 3.

Orc: Big Un no longer has Mighty Blow and now costs 100k.

Black Orc: Black Orc Move (MV) increased to 5; now costs 110k. Team moved to Tier 1.

Shambling Undead and Necromantic: Zombie Lineman access to 'D' is now secondary.

Chaos Dwarf: Hobgoblin Lineman now costs 40k. Chaos Dwarf Flamesmith lost Brawler and now costs 80k. Chaos Dwarf Blocker costs 65k. Bull Centaur gained Leap and Horns; now costs 150k. Players with the Dwarf tag lost access to 'A' (short legs, excluding Bull Centaur, as they have legs from a different creature). Team moved to Tier 1.

Dwarf: Dwarf Blocker Lineman: 'S' is now primary access, 'D' is secondary. Deathroller now costs 180k. Team moved to Tier 1.

Khorne: Bloodborn Marauder Lineman: PA reduced to 6+, AV increased to 9+, gained Brawler, and now costs 60k. Team moved to Tier 1.

Old World Alliance: Old World Dwarf Lineman: 'S' is now primary access, 'D' is secondary. Team moved to Tier 1.

Tomb Kings: Ancient Skeleton Lineman now has primary access to 'D' (remembers old strategies). Blitz-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 65k. Thro-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 85k. Team moved to Tier 1. Brawling Brutes tag removed.

Bretonnia: Bretonnian Squire now costs 50k.

Kislev: Lineman lost Fend, AV increased to 9+, now costs 50k. Kicker lost Fend and gained Punt. Wings PA lowered to 4+, lost Catch, gained Diving Catch, now costs 80k. Werebear removed from the team. New player Trained Bear added. Team gained Passing Virtuosos tag. Team moved to Tier 1.

Vampire: Thrall Lineman now costs 40k. Vampire Thrower now costs 115k. Apothecaries are banned.

Skills

Armbar forbids any dodge rerolls.

Shadowing is now a General skill.

Frenzy is now optional; the skill is declared immediately before the first block action in an activation.

Throw Teammate is now a passive skill.

Monstrous Mouth: Allows holding no more than 1 player. Exception: if used with Multiple Block, then up to 2 can be held.

Big Hand: In addition to old wording, now allows picking up the ball while in an adjacent square (but not while in an opponent's control zone).

Disturbing Presence: Works on ball pickup (still does not affect Secure the Ball). Does not work when the player is prone.

Foul Appearance: Now triggers on a 1-2 and does not end the player's activation upon triggering.

Iron Hard Skin: Now grants +1 to AV in addition to basic properties.

Tentacles: Now simply works on a raw roll of 4+ regardless of model strength.

New Mutation - Bone Hook: Before performing a foul action, you may move the target player to 1 square adjacent to the active player (occurs before calculating assists).

Leap: Now always works on an unmodifiable 4+ roll (improved by Very Long Legs mutation).

New General Skill - Elvenball: Allows movement after a Secure the Ball action.

Guard: Now only works during the opponent's turn.

Offensive (New General Skill): Grants an unblockable assist for a block during your turn.

Defensive: Now cancels the Offensive skill (functionally unchanged).

Stab: Removed from the skill list; it is now a trait, as it was before.

Shiv (New Skill): Works like Stab, but can only be used once per turn among all players with Shiv.

Bloodlust: New wording. Immediately after declaring any action by a player with this rule, roll a d6: on a 2+, the turn continues normally. If a 1 is rolled, the player must go "have a snack." The action is lost, and the player may choose a teammate without the Vampire or Undead tags in an adjacent square to roll for injury against, treating any result on the injury table as Badly Hurt. This roll causes a Turnover only if the Thrall was holding the ball. Before the bite, the player may declare movement. After the bite, the player's activation ends. If the bite is not performed, a Turnover occurs.

Breathe Fire: No longer causes Knocked Down on a roll of 1.

Brawler: Cannot be used in negative dice blocks.

Elite Skills: Deleted. Instead, the concept of "Elite Combos" is added. Having one on a player adds +15k to their cost. The following combos are elite: Claws+Mighty Blow, Guard+Defensive, Wrestle+Evasive, Sneaky Git+Dirty Player.

Diving Catch and On the Ball now work when Punt is declared.

Kick: Changed. Instead of a d6 roll, halve the d6 value during kickoff, rounding down. The final value can be 0.

Leader: Triggers at the start of a drive.

Inducements

Nuffle's Prayers now cost 15k.

Biased Referee now matches the rulebook wording. Instead of rerolling 1s, it grants +1 to disputing rolls.

Frolicking Nurgling now grants +1 Cheerleader and Assistant Coach until the end of the match; costs 15k.

Added new inducement: Giant.

Morg 'n' Thorg has been reworked; check the Star Player table; he is now truly powerful.

Bloodweiser Keg now costs 40k.

Cheerleaders now cost 10k (bear with it; it's justified by upcoming season rules).

Weather Mage now costs 20k.

Additional Training now costs 85k.

Free Agent removed from the available inducements list.

WAAAGH! Drummer now rolls 4d6 and allows moving your own players; chain pushes are forbidden.

Grape Day bottles now cost 30k.

Mark of Chaos - Khorne: Now grants Piledriver and Jump Up.

Favoured Of...

Addition to the second paragraph: If a player has 'M' access in secondary skills or none at all, they must choose a skill randomly.

Now rolls on a d8. Slaanesh triggers on 6, Nurgle on 7, Khorne on 8, Tzeentch on 9(6), Hashut on 4.

Undivided: Skill list replaced with: Prehensile Tail, Extra Arm, Disturbing Presence.

Tzeentch: Skill list replaced with: Two Heads, Extra Arm, Very Long Legs.

Khorne: Skill list replaced with: Horns, Iron Hard Skin, Prehensile Tail.

Slaanesh: Skill list replaced with: Tentacles, Foul Appearance, Extra Arm.

Nurgle: Skill list replaced with: Tentacles, Monstrous Mouth, Bone Hook.

Hashut: Skill list replaced with: Iron Hard Skin, Horns, Bone Hook.

pages.title: Accurate
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player makes a Quick Pass or Short Pass, they may add +1 to the Passing Ability test.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Accurate and Cannoneer are merged into one skill: Accurate. This player gains +1 to all pass attempts.

pages.title: Arm Bar
pages.body:
## Rule summary
If an opponent Falls Over while Dodging, Jumping or Leaping away from this player's tackle zone, this player may add +1 to that opponent's Armour or Injury roll. If that causes a Casualty, this player is credited for SPP.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Opposing players in this player's tackle zone cannot use rerolls on Dodge tests. If an opponent is Knocked Out or injured while dodging away from this player, this player gains SPP as if they had caused the result with Block.

pages.title: Big Hand
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player ignores negative modifiers when attempting to pick up the ball.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player ignores negative modifiers when attempting to pick up the ball. They may also pick up the ball from an adjacent square, but not while in an opponent's control zone.

pages.title: Block
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When a Both Down result is applied in a Block involving this player, they may choose not to be Knocked Down.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change

- Cannoneer is no longer a separate skill. If a roster, rule, or older reference points to Cannoneer, use Accurate instead: the player gains +1 to all pass attempts.

pages.title: Claws
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player Knocks Down an opponent during a Block Action, a natural armour roll of 8+ breaks armour regardless of the target's Armour value.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Claws, Mighty Blow, and Pile Driver fully stack. When Claws and Mighty Blow are used together, any Armour roll of 7+ breaks armour.

  • Claws, Mighty Blow, and Pile Driver fully stack. When Claws and Mighty Blow are used together, any Armour roll of 7+ breaks armour.

pages.body:

When blocking a stronger opponent, roll D6 and add this player's ST. If the total is higher than the opponent's unmodified ST, treat this player's unmodified ST as equal to the opponent's for that block.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.body:

Opponents within 3 squares of this player suffer -1 per such player when passing, throwing a team-mate or bomb, catching, or intercepting.

## Gata League change
- Disturbing Presence affects ball pickup rolls, but still does not affect Secure the Ball. It does not work while this player is Prone.

pages.body:

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use Diving Catch for Passes, Throw-ins, Kick-offs, and when Punt is declared.

pages.title: Diving Tackle
pages.body:

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Frenzy
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player blocks and Pushes Back a standing target, they must Follow-up if able and then make a second Block against the same target if possible. It cannot be combined with Grab, Hit and Run, or Multiple Block.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Frenzy is optional. It must be declared immediately before the first Block action of this player's activation. If declared and the first Block pushes back a standing target, resolve Frenzy's follow-up Block as normal.

pages.title: Fumblerooskie
pages.body:
## Rule summary
During a Move Action while carrying the ball, this player may place the ball on the ground in a square they move out of without causing a Turnover.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Fumblerooskie is an Agility skill. During a Move action while carrying the ball, this player may place the ball on the ground in a square they move out of without causing a Turnover.

pages.title: Give and Go
pages.body:
## Rule summary
After this player makes a Quick Pass or Hand-off without causing a Turnover, their activation does not end; they may keep moving with any remaining movement.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Grab
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player blocks and Pushes Back an opponent, this player's coach may choose any available adjacent square for the push. Opponents cannot use Sidestep against this player's Block Actions, and this skill cannot be combined with Frenzy.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Guard
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may provide Offensive and Defensive Assists for Blocks even while Marked by opposing players.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- During the opponent's turn, this player may provide block assists even while Marked. Guard does not work during your own turn.

pages.title: Hail Mary Pass
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may target any square on the pitch with a Pass or Throw Bomb action, treating the throw as a Long Bomb. Accurate results become inaccurate, and the throw cannot be intercepted.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Hit and Run
pages.body:
## Rule summary
After this player completes a Block Action or Stab Special Action and remains Standing, they may move one free square ignoring tackle zones, ending clear of all opposing marks. This skill cannot be combined with Frenzy.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.body:

When this player declares a Blitz Action, they add +1 ST for any Block Actions made during that Blitz.

pages.title: Iron Hard Skin
pages.body:

Opponents cannot apply Armour-roll modifiers against this player, and Claws cannot be used against them.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Iron Hard Skin grants +1 AV in addition to its basic properties. Its modifier-ignore effect only works during a Block.

pages.title: Juggernaut
pages.body:
## Rule summary
During a Blitz Action, this player may treat Both Down as Push Back on their Block Actions. Targets of those Blitz blocks cannot use Fend, Stand Firm or Wrestle.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- During this player's Blitz blocks, Juggernaut applies as normal and also cancels Foul Appearance.

pages.title: Jump Up
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may stand up from Prone for free. They may also declare a Block Action while Prone; pass an Agility test with +1 to stand and block, or fail and end the activation Prone.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may stand up from Prone without spending movement. Jump Up also allows this player to replace Block with special actions such as Stab and to declare active skills while Prone.

pages.title: Kick
pages.body:
## Rule summary
If this player is the nominated kicker, the coach may reduce kick deviation from D6 squares to D3 squares.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- If this player is the nominated kicker, resolve kick deviation by halving the D6 value and rounding down instead of rolling D3. The final result can be 0.

pages.title: Leader
pages.body:
## Rule summary
While at least one player with Leader is available on the pitch at the relevant start timing, the team may gain one Leader re-roll. It is lost if all Leader players leave play before it is used.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- At the start of a drive, if a player with Leader is available on the pitch, the team gains its Leader reroll as normal.

pages.title: Leap
pages.body:
## Rule summary
During movement, this player may Leap over one adjacent square. Resolve it like a Jump, but reduce the negative modifier by 1, to a minimum of -1. This skill cannot be combined with Pogo.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Leap always works on an unmodifiable 4+ roll. Very Long Legs can improve this roll.

pages.title: Lethal Flight
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player is thrown by Throw Team-mate and knocks down an opponent on landing or bouncing, they may add +1 to either Armour or Injury. Casualties caused this way award SPP to the thrown player. Requires Right Stuff.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.body:

When this player Fouls with no Offensive or Defensive Assists involved, they may re-roll a failed Armour roll.

pages.title: Mighty Blow (+1)
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player Knocks Down an opponent during a Block Action, they may add +1 to either the Armour roll or Injury roll after seeing the roll.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- When this player applies Mighty Blow (+1), it can fully stack with Claws and Pile Driver. With Claws and Mighty Blow together, any Armour roll of 7+ breaks armour.

pages.title: Monstrous Mouth
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Chomp Special Action against a standing opponent they Mark. On 3+, the opponent is Chomped and cannot leave while still Marked by this player. Strip Ball also cannot be used against this player.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Monstrous Mouth can hold no more than one player. If used with Multiple Block, it can hold up to two players.

pages.title: Multiple Block
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may Block two different adjacent opponents at once, reducing their ST by 2 for the blocks. They cannot Follow-up, and the skill cannot be combined with Frenzy.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use Multiple Block to affect two adjacent opponents as normal. Multiple Block also works on special actions that replace Block.

pages.title: On the Ball
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When an opponent declares a Pass Action, this player may move up to 3 squares before the pass roll, without Rushing. One open receiving player with this skill may also move up to 3 squares after kick deviation and before the kick-off event, unless a touchback occurs.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use On the Ball when an opponent declares a Pass or Punt, and when resolving the normal kick-off movement from this skill.

pages.body:
## Rule summary
At the end of each opponent turn, roll for Prone team-mates within 3 squares of a standing player with this rule. On 5+, the Prone player may immediately stand up.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Pile Driver
pages.body:
## Rule summary
After this player Knocks Down an opponent during a Block Action, they may make a free Foul Action against that opponent if still standing and Marking them, then this player becomes Prone and their activation ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- After an Armour roll from a Block action, this player may become Placed Prone to roll that Armour roll again. This does not work with skills that replace Block. Pile Driver is a Strength skill and fully stacks with Claws and Mighty Blow.

pages.title: Pro
pages.body:
## Rule summary
During this player's activation, they may try to re-roll one eligible die, dice pool or die from a multi-dice roll. On 3+ the re-roll is allowed; after trying Pro, the same roll cannot be re-rolled from another source.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Punt
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Punt Special Action after moving. If carrying the ball, kick it using the throw-in template for direction and distance; the ball bouncing loose is not a Turnover, but the crowd or an opposing catch is.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may give an Offensive Assist to a team-mate's Foul Action even while Marked by opposing players.

pages.body:

After this player performs a Foul Action, their activation does not end and they may continue moving with any movement they still have.

pages.title: Regenearation
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player suffers a Casualty, roll before the Casualty roll. On 4+ the casualty is ignored and the player goes to Reserves; on 1-3, resolve the casualty normally.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Saboteur
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player is Knocked Down by an opposing Block Action, roll before their Armour roll. On 4+ the sabotaged weapon also Knocks Down the attacker, then this player is automatically Knocked Out. Requires Secret Weapon.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Safe Pair of Hands
pages.path: Skills and Traits/Skills/Safe Pair of Hands.md
pages.body:
## Rule summary
If this player would become Prone while holding the ball, they may place the ball in an adjacent empty square before becoming Prone instead of letting it bounce.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Safe Pass
pages.body:
## Rule summary
If this player rolls a natural 1 on a Passing Ability test, the pass is not fumbled. The player keeps the ball, their activation ends, and no Turnover is caused.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Safe Pass protects against any Fumble, not only a natural 1.

pages.title: Secret Weapon. Loner (4+)
pages.body:
## Rule summary
Combined roster shorthand. Secret Weapon means the player is Sent-off at the end of a drive in which they took part; Loner (4+) means they must roll 4+ to use a Team re-roll.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/
pages.html:

pages.title: Shadowing
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When an opponent Dodges out of this player's tackle zone, this player may roll. On 4+ they move into the square the opponent left. Uses per turn are limited by this player's MA.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Shadowing is a General skill. When an opponent dodges out of this player's tackle zone, this player may roll to follow them into the square they left, up to the normal movement limit for Shadowing.
pages.html:
Rule summary

When an opponent Dodges out of this player's tackle zone, this player may roll. On 4+ they move into the square the opponent left. Uses per turn are limited by this player's MA.

- New Gata League Devious skill.

pages.body:

Gata trait. At the start of activation, after any Very Stupid check, this player may hide in or come out of its shell. While hidden, MA becomes 0 and AV becomes 11+, and the player cannot receive adjacent-player bonuses for Very Stupid checks.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Sneaky Git
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player Fouls and rolls natural doubles on the Armour roll, they are not Sent-off if the target's armour is not broken. If armour is broken, the normal sending-off rules apply.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- When this player fouls and breaks armour, they are no longer sent off for rolling doubles on the Armour roll.

pages.body:

pages.body:

Whenever this player would be Knocked Down or Fall Over, roll a D6. On 6, they remain standing; if this happens during their activation, they may continue and no Turnover is caused.

pages.title: Tackle
pages.body:

Opponents Dodging away from this player's tackle zone cannot use Dodge. When this player Blocks, the target cannot count Dodge against Stumble results. In Gata League, Tackle also cancels Evasive.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- When this player Blocks an opponent, the target cannot use Evasive against that Block.

pages.title: Tentacles
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When an opponent tries to Dodge, Jump or Leap away from this player, this player may roll using ST to hold them in place. Success ends the opponent's movement in the square they tried to leave.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Tentacles works on a raw 4+ roll, regardless of the players' Strength values.

- Thick Skull is a General skill. This player treats applicable Knocked-out Injury results as Stunned instead.

pages.title: Very Stupid
pages.body:
## Rule summary
Use Really Stupid-style activation. After declaring an action, roll D6; with a suitable adjacent standing team-mate the roll gets +2. On 4+ act normally; on failure the player becomes Distracted.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Wrestle
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player is involved in a Block and Both Down is applied, they may choose to place both players Prone, ignoring skills that would otherwise keep either player standing.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Always Hungry
pages.body:
## Rule summary
Before throwing a team-mate, this player must roll. On 2+ continue normally; on 1, roll again. The second roll either makes the team-mate squirm free for a fumbled throw or be eaten and removed, causing a Turnover if they had the ball.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Ball & Chain
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player can only take a Ball and Chain Special Action. They move randomly with the throw-in template, automatically dodge away, crash into players, push prone players, bounce the ball, and risk Injury by the Crowd if they leave the pitch.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Bloodlust (X+)
pages.body:
## Rule summary
Before activation, roll against the listed Bloodlust target number, with +1 for Block or Blitz. On failure the player may continue, but must bite an adjacent Thrall Lineman by the relevant point or cause a Turnover and become Distracted.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Roll a D6 after declaring any action: on a 2+, the turn continues normally. On a 1, the player must feed. The action is lost; the player may choose an adjacent team-mate without the Vampire or Undead tags to perform an Injury roll against, treating all results as Badly Hurt. This roll causes a Turnover only if the bitten player was holding the ball. Movement may be declared before the bite; after the bite, this player's activation ends. If no bite is performed, a Turnover occurs.

pages.title: Bombardier
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Throw Bomb Special Action. Bombs are thrown like passes, explode if they land or are dropped, knock down standing players hit by the blast, and force a caught bomb to be thrown again immediately.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Breathe Fire
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Breathe Fire Special Action against a standing adjacent opponent. Roll D6 with -1 against ST 5+ targets; high results place the target Prone or Knocked Down, and the action ends the activation.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use Breathe Fire as a special action against an adjacent standing opponent. A roll of 1 does not cause Knocked Down.

pages.title: Chainsaw
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Chainsaw Attack Special Action against an adjacent opponent, usually making an Armour roll with +3. On a kick-back or if this player is knocked down/falls over, the chainsaw makes them much easier to injure. It can also be used on Fouls.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.body:
## Rule summary
Apply +1 to Casualty rolls made against this player.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- When this player receives an Injury roll, roll two dice and choose the highest result.

pages.title: Hypnotic Gaze
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Hypnotic Gaze Special Action after moving. Choose an adjacent standing opponent; on a successful roll that opponent becomes Distracted and this player's activation ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Once per turn, this player may use Hypnotic Gaze as a special action against an adjacent standing opponent. Resolve the gaze as normal; on success, the target becomes Distracted. No team may use Hypnotic Gaze more than once in the same turn.

pages.title: Insignificant
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When building a roster, you cannot include more players with Insignificant than players without it.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Kick Team-Mate
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Kick Team-mate Special Action. It works like Throw Team-mate, but does not use the team's Throw Team-mate action for the turn; a fumbled kick injures the kicked player more harshly.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: My Ball
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player may not willingly give up the ball. They cannot Pass, Hand-off, or use abilities that voluntarily relinquish possession; they lose it only by being taken down or by an opponent's effect.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: No Ball
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player can never possess the ball, automatically fails attempts to catch or pick it up, and cannot attempt interceptions.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: No Hands
pages.body:
## Rule summary
This player cannot take possession of the ball. Any catch, pick-up or interception attempt automatically fails as if a natural 1 had been rolled.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Pick-Me-Up
pages.body:
## Rule summary
At the end of each opponent turn, roll for Prone team-mates within 3 squares of a standing player with this rule. On 5+, the Prone player may immediately stand up.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/
pages.html:
Rule summary

At the end of each opponent turn, roll for Prone team-mates within 3 squares of a standing player with this rule. On 5+, the Prone player may immediately stand up.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Plague Ridden
pages.body:
## Rule summary
Once per game, if this player kills an eligible opposing player with a Block Action and the death is not saved, add a new Lineman from this team roster to Reserves. It may be hired after the game like a Journeyman.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- When a player with Plague Ridden or Masters of Undeath kills an opponent, their coach gains a Lineman from their own team. Shambling Undead may choose either a Skeleton or a Zombie.

pages.title: Pogo
pages.body:
## Rule summary
During movement, this player may Pogo over one adjacent square like a Jump while ignoring the usual negative jump modifiers. This trait cannot be combined with Leap.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- During this player's movement, they may use Pogo to jump over one adjacent square. Pogo can only be used once per activation.

pages.title: Projectile Vomit
pages.body:

This player may make a Projectile Vomit Special Action against an adjacent standing opponent. On 2+ make an unmodified Armour roll against the target; on 1 make the same unmodified Armour roll against this player.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.body:

After declaring an action, roll D6. Add +2 if an eligible standing non-Distracted team-mate is adjacent. On 4+ act normally; on 1-3 this player becomes Distracted.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Regeneration
pages.body:
## Rule summary
When this player suffers a Casualty, roll before the Casualty roll. On 4+ the casualty is ignored and the player goes to Reserves; on 1-3, resolve the casualty normally.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Secret Weapon
pages.body:
## Rule summary
At the end of any drive in which this player took part, they are Sent-off, even if they are no longer on the pitch when the drive ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- At the end of a drive, Secret Weapon only applies if this player was fielded for that drive.

pages.title: Snail Sh
pages.body:

Gata trait. At the start of activation, after any Very Stupid check, this player may hide in or come out of its shell. While hidden, MA becomes 0 and AV becomes 11+, and the player cannot receive adjacent-player bonuses for Very Stupid checks.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

pages.title: Stab
pages.body:

This player may make a Stab Special Action against an adjacent standing opponent. Make an unmodified Armour roll; if armour breaks, roll Injury. It can replace the Block in a Blitz, but then the activation ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use Stab as a special action against an adjacent standing opponent. During a Blitz, Stab may replace the Block action and no longer ends this player's movement.

pages.title: Take Root
pages.body:

When activated while standing, roll D6. On 2+ act normally; on 1 the player becomes Rooted, cannot move, follow up, be pushed back, or leave the square until knocked down, placed prone, removed, or the drive ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

skills.title: Accurate
skills.body:
## Rule summary
When this player makes a Quick Pass or Short Pass, they may add +1 to the Passing Ability test.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Accurate and Cannoneer are merged into one skill: Accurate. This player gains +1 to all pass attempts.

skills.title: Arm Bar
skills.body:
## Rule summary
If an opponent Falls Over while Dodging, Jumping or Leaping away from this player's tackle zone, this player may add +1 to that opponent's Armour or Injury roll. If that causes a Casualty, this player is credited for SPP.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Opposing players in this player's tackle zone cannot use rerolls on Dodge tests. If an opponent is Knocked Out or injured while dodging away from this player, this player gains SPP as if they had caused the result with Block.

skills.title: Big Hand
skills.body:
## Rule summary
This player ignores negative modifiers when attempting to pick up the ball.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player ignores negative modifiers when attempting to pick up the ball. They may also pick up the ball from an adjacent square, but not while in an opponent's control zone.

skills.title: Block
skills.body:
## Rule summary
When a Both Down result is applied in a Block involving this player, they may choose not to be Knocked Down.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- During this player's own activation, when a Both Down result is applied in a Block involving this player, this player is not Knocked Down by that result. Outside this player's own activation, Block does not apply.

skills.title: Bone Head
skills.body:
## Rule summary
After declaring an action, roll a D6. On 2+ the player acts normally; on 1 they become Distracted and the activation ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

skills.title: Bone Hook
skills.body:
## Rule summary
Gata mutation. Before this player performs a Foul Action, they may move the target player one square so that the target is adjacent to the active player; resolve this before counting foul assists.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- New Gata League mutation.

skills.title: Brawler
skills.body:
## Rule summary
When this player declares a Block Action, they may re-roll one Both Down result from that block.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Once during a Block, this player may reroll a single block die. Brawler cannot be used in negative dice blocks.

- This player may use Diving Catch for Passes, Throw-ins, Kick-offs, and when Punt is declared.

skills.body:

When this player blocks and Pushes Back a standing target, they must Follow-up if able and then make a second Block against the same target if possible. It cannot be combined with Grab, Hit and Run, or Multiple Block.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Frenzy is optional. It must be declared immediately before the first Block action of this player's activation. If declared and the first Block pushes back a standing target, resolve Frenzy's follow-up Block as normal.

skills.title: Hail Mary Pass
skills.body:
## Rule summary
This player may target any square on the pitch with a Pass or Throw Bomb action, treating the throw as a Long Bomb. Accurate results become inaccurate, and the throw cannot be intercepted.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

skills.title: Hit and Run
skills.body:
## Rule summary
After this player completes a Block Action or Stab Special Action and remains Standing, they may move one free square ignoring tackle zones, ending clear of all opposing marks. This skill cannot be combined with Frenzy.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

skills.body:

When this player declares a Blitz Action, they add +1 ST for any Block Actions made during that Blitz.

skills.title: Iron Hard Skin
skills.body:
## Rule summary
Opponents cannot apply Armour-roll modifiers against this player, and Claws cannot be used against them.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Iron Hard Skin grants +1 AV in addition to its basic properties. Its modifier-ignore effect only works during a Block.

skills.title: Juggernaut
skills.body:
## Rule summary
During a Blitz Action, this player may treat Both Down as Push Back on their Block Actions. Targets of those Blitz blocks cannot use Fend, Stand Firm or Wrestle.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- During this player's Blitz blocks, Juggernaut applies as normal and also cancels Foul Appearance.

skills.title: Jump Up
skills.body:
## Rule summary
This player may stand up from Prone for free. They may also declare a Block Action while Prone; pass an Agility test with +1 to stand and block, or fail and end the activation Prone.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may stand up from Prone without spending movement. Jump Up also allows this player to replace Block with special actions such as Stab and to declare active skills while Prone.

skills.title: Kick
skills.body:
## Rule summary
If this player is the nominated kicker, the coach may reduce kick deviation from D6 squares to D3 squares.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- If this player is the nominated kicker, resolve kick deviation by halving the D6 value and rounding down instead of rolling D3. The final result can be 0.

skills.title: Leader
skills.body:
## Rule summary
While at least one player with Leader is available on the pitch at the relevant start timing, the team may gain one Leader re-roll. It is lost if all Leader players leave play before it is used.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- At the start of a drive, if a player with Leader is available on the pitch, the team gains its Leader reroll as normal.

skills.title: Leap
skills.body:
## Rule summary
During movement, this player may Leap over one adjacent square. Resolve it like a Jump, but reduce the negative modifier by 1, to a minimum of -1. This skill cannot be combined with Pogo.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Leap always works on an unmodifiable 4+ roll. Very Long Legs can improve this roll.

skills.title: Lethal Flight
skills.body:

When this player is thrown by Throw Team-mate and knocks down an opponent on landing or bouncing, they may add +1 to either Armour or Injury. Casualties caused this way award SPP to the thrown player. Requires Right Stuff.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

skills.title: Mighty Blow
skills.body:
## Rule summary
When this player Knocks Down an opponent during a Block Action, they may add +1 to either the Armour roll or Injury roll after seeing the roll.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- When this player applies Mighty Blow, it can fully stack with Claws and Pile Driver. With Claws and Mighty Blow together, any Armour roll of 7+ breaks armour.
