skills.title: Monstrous Mouth
skills.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Chomp Special Action against a standing opponent they Mark. On 3+, the opponent is Chomped and cannot leave while still Marked by this player. Strip Ball also cannot be used against this player.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Monstrous Mouth can hold no more than one player. If used with Multiple Block, it can hold up to two players.

skills.title: Multiple Block
skills.body:
## Rule summary
This player may Block two different adjacent opponents at once, reducing their ST by 2 for the blocks. They cannot Follow-up, and the skill cannot be combined with Frenzy.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use Multiple Block to affect two adjacent opponents as normal. Multiple Block also works on special actions that replace Block.

skills.title: On the Ball
skills.body:
## Rule summary
When an opponent declares a Pass Action, this player may move up to 3 squares before the pass roll, without Rushing. One open receiving player with this skill may also move up to 3 squares after kick deviation and before the kick-off event, unless a touchback occurs.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use On the Ball when an opponent declares a Pass or Punt, and when resolving the normal kick-off movement from this skill.

At the end of each opponent turn, roll for Prone team-mates within 3 squares of a standing player with this rule. On 5+, the Prone player may immediately stand up.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

skills.body:

After this player Knocks Down an opponent during a Block Action, they may make a free Foul Action against that opponent if still standing and Marking them, then this player becomes Prone and their activation ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- After an Armour roll from a Block action, this player may become Placed Prone to roll that Armour roll again. This does not work with skills that replace Block. Pile Driver is a Strength skill and fully stacks with Claws and Mighty Blow.

skills.title: Pro
skills.body:
## Rule summary
During this player's activation, they may try to re-roll one eligible die, dice pool or die from a multi-dice roll. On 3+ the re-roll is allowed; after trying Pro, the same roll cannot be re-rolled from another source.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/
skills.html:
Rule summary

During this player's activation, they may try to re-roll one eligible die, dice pool or die from a multi-dice roll. On 3+ the re-roll is allowed; after trying Pro, the same roll cannot be re-rolled from another source.

skills.title: Safe Pass
skills.body:

If this player rolls a natural 1 on a Passing Ability test, the pass is not fumbled. The player keeps the ball, their activation ends, and no Turnover is caused.

## Gata League change
- Safe Pass protects against any Fumble, not only a natural 1.

skills.title: Shadowing
skills.body:
## Rule summary
When an opponent Dodges out of this player's tackle zone, this player may roll. On 4+ they move into the square the opponent left. Uses per turn are limited by this player's MA.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Shadowing is a General skill. When an opponent dodges out of this player's tackle zone, this player may roll to follow them into the square they left, up to the normal movement limit for Shadowing.

- When this player fouls and breaks armour, they are no longer sent off for rolling doubles on the Armour roll.

skills.title: Wrestle
skills.body:
## Rule summary
When this player is involved in a Block and Both Down is applied, they may choose to place both players Prone, ignoring skills that would otherwise keep either player standing.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

traits.title: Always Hungry
traits.body:
## Rule summary
Before throwing a team-mate, this player must roll. On 2+ continue normally; on 1, roll again. The second roll either makes the team-mate squirm free for a fumbled throw or be eaten and removed, causing a Turnover if they had the ball.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

traits.title: Ball & Chain
traits.body:
## Rule summary
This player can only take a Ball and Chain Special Action. They move randomly with the throw-in template, automatically dodge away, crash into players, push prone players, bounce the ball, and risk Injury by the Crowd if they leave the pitch.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

traits.title: Bloodlust
traits.body:
## Rule summary
Before activation, roll against the listed Bloodlust target number, with +1 for Block or Blitz. On failure the player may continue, but must bite an adjacent Thrall Lineman by the relevant point or cause a Turnover and become Distracted.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Roll a D6 after declaring any action: on a 2+, the turn continues normally. On a 1, the player must feed. The action is lost; the player may choose an adjacent team-mate without the Vampire or Undead tags to perform an Injury roll against, treating all results as Badly Hurt. This roll causes a Turnover only if the bitten player was holding the ball. Movement may be declared before the bite; after the bite, this player's activation ends. If no bite is performed, a Turnover occurs.

traits.title: Bombardier
traits.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Throw Bomb Special Action. Bombs are thrown like passes, explode if they land or are dropped, knock down standing players hit by the blast, and force a caught bomb to be thrown again immediately.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

traits.title: Breathe Fire
traits.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Breathe Fire Special Action against a standing adjacent opponent. Roll D6 with -1 against ST 5+ targets; high results place the target Prone or Knocked Down, and the action ends the activation.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- This player may use Breathe Fire as a special action against an adjacent standing opponent. A roll of 1 does not cause Knocked Down.

traits.title: Chainsaw
traits.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Chainsaw Attack Special Action against an adjacent opponent, usually making an Armour roll with +3. On a kick-back or if this player is knocked down/falls over, the chainsaw makes them much easier to injure. It can also be used on Fouls.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

- When this player receives an Injury roll, roll two dice and choose the highest result.

traits.body:
## Rule summary
This player may make a Hypnotic Gaze Special Action after moving. Choose an adjacent standing opponent; on a successful roll that opponent becomes Distracted and this player's activation ends.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- Once per turn, this player may use Hypnotic Gaze as a special action against an adjacent standing opponent. Resolve the gaze as normal; on success, the target becomes Distracted. No team may use Hypnotic Gaze more than once in the same turn.

traits.title: Loner (X+)
traits.body:
## Rule summary
When this player wants to use a Team re-roll, roll D6. If the result meets or beats the listed target, the re-roll may be used; otherwise the re-roll is lost and the original result stands.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

traits.title: Plague Ridden
traits.body:
## Rule summary
Once per game, if this player kills an eligible opposing player with a Block Action and the death is not saved, add a new Lineman from this team roster to Reserves. It may be hired after the game like a Journeyman.

Full wording: https://bloodbowlbase.ru/bb2025/core_rules/skills_and_traits/

## Gata League change
- When a player with Plague Ridden or Masters of Undeath kills an opponent, their coach gains a Lineman from their own team. Shambling Undead may choose either a Skeleton or a Zombie.

## Gata League change
- During this player's movement, they may use Pogo to jump over one adjacent square. Pogo can only be used once per activation.

rules.title: 1. League Basics
rules.body:
# 1. League Basics

Blood Bowl 2025 + homebrew

Gata League 2:  Info

Minis

Good vibes

A set of Dice

How to Be a Great Player

Play fair and with respect for your opponent.

Rollbacks are allowed for any actions, but only before the dice are thrown.

Control your frustration ("tilt") over the dice rolls.

Report match results to the organizer on time.

Ask the organizer immediately if you have any questions about the rules that you cannot find the answer to.

Notify in advance if you cannot play your match on time.

Enjoy the football!

rules.title: 2. Team Creation
rules.body:
# 2. Team Creation

Model Requirements

Free Starting Roster: As a personal incentive for participating, the Commissioner will provide you with 9 players-enough for a valid roster-from any 3D-printed team, completely free of charge!

Model Standards: A model must clearly represent a team player, have a base, and be of an appropriate size.

Identification: The player's number must be clearly marked on either the model or its base, matching the number listed on your team roster.

Thematic Accuracy: Models must depict Blood Bowl players; weapons like swords, generic armor, etc., are not permitted.

Conversions: Conversions are allowed.

Clarification: If you are unsure whether your model is suitable for play, please consult the Commissioner.

League Registration

How to Register: Simply message the Commissioner, and you will be assigned a team sheet in the league table.

Promotion: As a free promotion, the Commissioner will 3D-print 9 models for your first team for free!

New Team Creation

Starting Budget: Teams are drafted with 600,000 gold pieces in accordance with the base rules.

Roster Size: The team size is limited to 14 players.

Specialists: Remember that in Sevens, a team can field no more than 4 specialists per drive, so be sure to purchase at least 3 players with the Lineman tag.

Registration: Enter all data into the league table.

Model Requirements

Model Standards: A model must clearly represent a team player, have a base, and be of an appropriate size.

Identification: The player's number must be clearly marked on either the model or its base, matching the number listed on your team roster.

Thematic Accuracy: Models must depict Blood Bowl players; weapons like swords, generic armor, etc., are not permitted.

Conversions: Conversions are allowed.

Clarification: If you are unsure whether your model is suitable for play, please consult the Commissioner.

Painting: It is highly encouraged to play with a painted team. A miniature is considered painted if all details are highlighted, base shadows and highlights have been applied, and the base is finished.

League Registration

How to Register: Simply message the Commissioner; you will be assigned a team sheet in the league table and added to the league chat.

Fees: Participation in the league is free, although donations for prizes and various fun additions are welcome..

Team Creation

New Roster: Teams are drafted with 600,000 gold pieces in accordance with the base rules.

Roster Size: The team size is limited to 14 players.

Specialists: Remember that in Sevens, a team can field no more than 4 specialists per drive, so be sure to purchase at least 3 players with the Lineman tag.

Registration: Enter all data into the league table.

Player Costs: Player costs have been changed compared to original Blood Bowl; please refer to Gata League 2: Info

Painting: It is highly encouraged to play with a painted team. A miniature is considered painted if all details are highlighted, base shadows and highlights have been applied, and the base is finished.

League Registration

How to Register: Simply message the Commissioner; you will be assigned a team sheet in the league table and added to the league chat.

Fees: Participation in the league is free, although donations for prizes and various fun additions are welcome..

Team Creation

New Roster: Teams are drafted with 600,000 gold pieces in accordance with the base rules.

Roster Size: The team size is limited to 14 players.

Specialists: Remember that in Sevens, a team can field no more than 4 specialists per drive, so be sure to purchase at least 3 players with the Lineman tag.

Registration: Enter all data into the league table.

Player Costs: Player costs have been changed compared to original Blood Bowl; please refer to Gata League 2: Info
rules.text: # 2. Team Creation Model Requirements Free Starting Roster: As a personal incentive for participating, the Commissioner will provide you with 9 players-enough for a valid roster-from any 3D-printed team, completely free of charge! Model Standards: A model must clearly represent a team player, have a base, and be of an appropriate size. Identification: The player's number must be clearly marked on either the model or its base, matching the number listed on your team roster. Thematic Accuracy: Models must depict Blood Bowl players; weapons like swords, generic armor, etc., are not permitted. Conversions: Conversions are allowed. Clarification: If you are unsure whether your model is suitable for play, please consult the Commissioner. rules.title: 3. Team Management
rules.body:
# 3. Team Management

Team Management

Team Transfer: You may transfer a team from the previous season without any restrictions.

Selling Players: Players can be sold after a match for half their price.

Roster Constraints: You cannot sell or remove a player if doing so would leave the team with fewer than 7 players.

Contracts

Contract Terms: Upon team formation, the coach signs a contract with the team. The contract renewal time is announced by the Commissioner and typically occurs at the end of the season.

Team Buyout: To purchase the team from the league treasury, 600,000 gold will be allocated, plus the funds in the team's treasury at the time of renewal, up to a total maximum of 900,000 gold.

Agent Fees: A player who has completed a contract will incur an additional 20,000 gold in agent fees for each contract completed after the first. For example, after 3 successful contracts, a player will cost 40,000 more to buy out. This increase is not counted toward the total team value.

Player Departure: Players who do not sign a contract leave the team.

Retention: After signing a new contract, a player retains all experience, acquired skills, and injuries.

Dissolution: You may dissolve your team and choose not to renew the contract if you wish.

Contract Renewal

Health Recovery:

Healing Niggling Injuries: Roll a d6 for each Niggling Injury; add a +1 modifier if you have an Apothecary. On a 4+, the vacation and rest work wonders, and the injury is successfully healed. If you fail, the injury remains with the player.

Healing Serious Injuries: Roll a d6 for each stat reduction on the player; add a +1 modifier if you have an Apothecary. On a 4+, the treatment is successful, the characteristic is restored to its previous value, and the player receives a Niggling Injury. If you fail, the injury remains with the player.

Removing Hatred: Roll a d6 for each acquired Hatred skill on the player. On a 4+, the player forgets their old grudges, and the skill is removed from the player's profile.

Staff & Personnel:

Assistant coaches, cheerleaders, and apothecaries go on unpaid leave.

Rerolls are lost.

Select the players you wish to keep on the team and pay their cost, plus any applicable agent fees.

Remove the remaining players. Count the number of upgrades the departing players had and roll that many d6s; for every 1-2 rolled, you lose one Fan Factor.

Purchase new players, staff, and rerolls as needed.

Season Structure and Scoring

Match Conduct:

Rounds: One round lasts two weeks, starting on a Monday and ending on a Sunday.

Deadlines: Each match must be played within its designated round.

Locations: Games can be played at the Litch Club, at Ded Max's Painting Evenings, or at someone's home by prior arrangement.

Virtual Play: If it is impossible to play in real life, the match can be played using the Tabletop Simulator (TTS) mod.

Scheduling: Time proposals for matches must be submitted by the end of Friday. If a coach only provides scheduling proposals over the weekend and the match is ultimately not played, the Commissioner will favor the coach who provided their availability in advance.

Incomplete Matches: If a match is not played within the round, the result will be determined by the Commissioner.

Season Start: At the beginning of the season, all missed game penalties are removed from all players.

League Points:

3 points for a win.

1 point for a draw.

2 points for a technical win.

0 points for a loss.

+1 point if the game ends with a margin of 3+ touchdowns.

+1 point if you conceded 0 touchdowns (you must score at least one yourself).

+2 points if the opponent could not field a team for the match (e.g., due to too many injuries).

End-of-Season Prizes:

At the end of the season, all teams not in the top rankings receive winnings:

10k for each match played.

20k for each victory.

10k for each draw.

Rookie Protection:

For the first 3 games after a team's creation, all injuries inflicted on its players are counted as Badly Hurt.

Technical Win & Tournament Structure

Technical Win: The winning team is awarded 2 MVP rolls and (d3+2) * 10k gold.

Tournament Bracket: Matches are assigned using the Swiss system.

Tie-breakers: The Buchholz coefficient and head-to-head results are used for tie-breakers, followed by the total number of touchdowns and injuries.

Playoff Qualification: Depending on the number of participants, the players at the top of the standings will receive an automatic bye into the tournament's playoff winner's bracket

Additional Rules

Special Rules and Gameplay Changes

Prohibited Inducements: Special Hired Wizards, Biased Referees, and Famous Coaching Staff are not used in the league. DOCX

Rules Adjustments: Some rules from the original Sevens and Blood Bowl have been modified for more enjoyable and deliberate gameplay. Most player costs have been recalculated; these changes can be viewed in the team table. DOCX+ 1

General Gameplay Rules

MVP Selection: Choose 3 players and roll a d3 to decide which one receives 5 SPP. DOCX

Kick-off: When the ball scatters on kick-off, roll 1d6 and halve the result (rounding down); the result can be zero. DOCX

Team Rerolls: The cost of Team Rerolls is not doubled during team drafting, but it is doubled if purchased during the season. DOCX

SPP for Knock Outs: A player who knocks out an opponent receives 1 SPP. DOCX

Experience (SPP):

Actions similar to Block or their consequences grant SPP according to current rules (1 for KO, 2 for Injury). For example, Stab, Chainsaw, Bombardier, and pushing a player off the pitch can now grant experience. DOCX+ 1

A Foul action grants experience just like a Block. DOCX

A Throw Teammate action grants the thrower experience for inflicting Injuries and KOs, just like a Block. DOCX

Inducement Tiering: When determining inducement budgets, the team's tier is considered. Tier 2 teams receive 200k when playing against Tier 1; Tier 3 teams receive 300k against Premier League teams or 100k against Tier 2 teams. DOCX+ 1

Injury Table: Use the injury table from the "big format" (standard) Blood Bowl. DOCX

Setup: The team setup is moved 3 squares forward. Sevens now has a Line of Scrimmage. You must place at least 3 players on the Line of Scrimmage in the central segment. No more than 1 player is allowed in each side segment. DOCX+ 3

Touchdown Restrictions: A touchdown cannot be scored on the first turn of any drive. If your player is in the opponent's end zone at the end of your first turn, you do not score, but if they are still there at the start of your second turn, the drive ends immediately and you score a touchdown. Exception: A touchdown can be scored on the first turn of a drive if the player was thrown via Throw Teammate. DOCX+ 2

Leaping: Jumping over prone players is prohibited unless the player has the Leap skill. DOCX

Mega-Stars: Star Players with the Mega-Star tag occupy both available Star Player slots. DOCX

Bribes: Bribes trigger automatically. DOCX

Secure the Ball!: The "no ball pickup" restriction radius is reduced to 1 square. DOCX

Bribery and Corruption: Allows purchasing up to 3 bribes per team for 50k each, which increase Team Value (TV). DOCX

TV Exceptions: Cheerleaders and assistant coaches do not count toward Team Value. DOCX

Painting Bonus: A fully painted team receives an additional 30k after a match. DOCX

Throw-Ins: Throw-ins are resolved using 1d6.

Team Changes

Necromantic Horror, Shambling Undead, and Tomb Kings: Gained access to hire a Mortuary Assistant (allows a regeneration reroll once per game) for 50k gold.

Nurgle: Gained access to hire a Plague Doctor (allows a regeneration reroll once per game, or acts as an apothecary) for 50k gold.

Team Captain: All teams now have the Team Captain special rule.

Ogre: May designate an Ogre Runt Punter player as captain.

Brawling Brutes: Some teams have gained the Brawling Brutes rule; refer to the team table for details.

Passing Virtuosos: Some teams have gained the new Passing Virtuosos special rule. Teams with this rule receive 2 SPP for touchdowns, and a successful catch during a pass grants the receiver 1 SPP upon completion.

Slann: Now a Tier 1 team. All Slann players replace the Pogo skill with Leap.

Halfling: Gained access to the Old World Classic league.

Goblin: Pogoer, Ooligan, and Doom Diver players now have the Lineman tag.

Chaos Chosen: Chaos Blocker players now have Iron Hard Skin.

Norse: The Beer Boar player now has the Lineman tag; the Norse Raider Lineman no longer has the Unsteady rule.

High Elf: Updated according to current rules, and costs have been revised.

Elven Union: Elf Lineman players have lost the Fumblerooskie skill.

Gnome: The team list has been completely reworked.

Orc: The Big Un no longer has the Mighty Blow skill.

Black Orc: Black Orc movement (MV) increased to 5, and their cost is now 110k. The team has been moved to Tier 1.

Chaos Dwarf: The team has been reworked.

Dwarf: Dwarf Blocker Lineman now have 'S' as primary access and 'D' as secondary access. The Deathroller now costs 180k, and the team has been moved to Tier 1.

Khorne: The Bloodborn Marauder Lineman has had their PA reduced to 6+, AV increased to 9+, gained the Brawler skill, and now costs 60k. The team has been moved to Tier 1.

Tomb Kings: Ancient Skeleton Lineman players now have primary access to 'D'. Blitz-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 65k. Thro-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 85k. The team has been moved to Tier 1, and the Brawling Brutes tag has been removed.

Kislev: The team has been reworked.

Vampire: Thrall Lineman now cost 40k. Vampire Thrower now cost 115k. It is now forbidden to hire an apothecary for the team.

:

Skill Changes

Claws, Mighty Blow, and Piledriver: These skills now fully stack (with Claws and Mighty Blow, you break any armor on a 7+).

Piledriver: Completely changed. After an armor roll from a Block action, the player using the skill can become Placed Prone to roll again. This does not work with skills that replace Block and has been moved to the Strength category.

Hypnotic Gaze: Partially changed; can now only be used once per turn.

Brawler: Completely changed; allows for a one-time reroll of a single die during a Block. Cannot be used in negative dice blocks.

Plague Ridden/Masters of Undeath: Completely changed; when a player with this rule kills an opponent, their coach gains a Lineman of their own team (Shambling Undead can choose between a Skeleton or a Zombie).

Throw Teammate: Now a passive skill.

Blood Lust: New wording. Roll a d6 after declaring any action: on a 2+, the turn continues normally. On a 1, the player must "feed." The action is lost; the player may choose an adjacent teammate (without the Vampire or Undead tags) to perform an injury roll against, treating all results as Badly Hurt. This roll causes a turnover only if the Thrall was holding the ball. Movement may be declared before the bite; after the bite, the player's activation ends. If no bite is performed, a turnover occurs.

Accurate and Cannoneer: Merged into one skill (Accurate), which provides +1 to all passes.

Dodge: Completely changed; now only allows a reroll for dodge tests.

Evasive (New): The player can treat a result of 5 as 3-4 when being blocked, and Stumble results as Push Back.

Tackle: Completely changed; it now ignores the Evasive skill.

Arm Bar: Completely changed; prevents rerolls on dodge tests for players in the owner's tackle zone. Grants SPP like Block if the opponent is injured or knocked out while dodging away.

Block: Partially changed; only works during the player's own activation.

Thick Skull: Moved to the General category.

Sneaky Git: You are no longer removed on doubles when fouling during an armor break.

Stab: No longer ends movement during a Blitz.

Shiv (New): Works like Stab but can only be used once per turn among all players with the skill.

Decay: Completely changed; when receiving an injury, roll two dice and choose the highest.

Safe Pass: Protects against any Fumble, not just on a natural 1.

Fumblerooski: Moved to the Agility category.

Pogo: Can only be used once per activation.

Secret Weapon: Only works if the player was fielded for the drive.

Shadowing: Now a General skill.

Frenzy: Now optional; must be declared immediately before the first block action of the activation.

Monstrous Mouth: Limits holding to 1 player (unless used with Multiple Block, then up to 2).

Big Hand: Additionally allows picking up the ball from an adjacent square (but not while in an opponent's control zone).

Disturbing Presence: Works on ball pickup (still does not affect Secure the Ball); does not work if the player is prone.

Foul Appearance: Now triggers on a 1-2 roll and does not end the player's activation.

Iron Hard Skin: Now grants +1 AV in addition to basic properties; the modifier ignore only works during a block.

Tentacles: Now works on a raw 4+ roll, regardless of the models' strength.

Bone Hook (New Mutation): Before a Foul action, you may move the target player 1 square to an adjacent cell (happens before assist calculation).

Leap: Now always works on an unmodifiable 4+ roll (improved by the Very Long Legs mutation).

Elvenball (New General Skill): Grants the ability to move after a Secure the Ball action.

Guard: Now only works during the opponent's turn.

Offensive (New General Skill): Grants an unblockable assist for a block during your turn.

Defensive: Now cancels the Offensive skill.

Breathe Fire: Does not cause Knocked Down on a roll of 1.

Diving Catch / On the Ball: Work when Punt is declared.

Jump Up: Allows replacing Block with special actions (e.g., Stab) and declaring active skills while Prone.

Multiple Block: Works on special actions that replace Block.

Juggernaut: Also cancels Foul Appearance.

Kick: Changed; instead of a d3 roll, halve the d6 value during kickoff (rounding down, result can be 0).

Leader: Triggers at the start of a drive.

Changes to "Favoured Of..."

Real Bonuses: The Favoured of rule now provides actual bonuses.

Deity Selection: If a team has a choice for this rule, you must select one of the following options during team registration: Undivided, Khorne, Nurgle, Tzeentch, Slaanesh, or Hashut.

Skill Acquisition: Every time a player receives an advancement, roll a d8.

Undivided triggers on 1.

Slaanesh triggers on 6.

Nurgle triggers on 7.

Khorne triggers on 8.

Tzeentch triggers on 9 (or 6).

Hashut triggers on 4.

Additional Skill: A player may receive one of the skills corresponding to your choice in addition to the one taken for a regular advancement. This skill increases the player's value, though it does not increase the player's rank.

Random Selection: If a player has 'M' access in secondary skills or no access at all, they must choose the skill randomly.

Skill Lists:

Undivided: Prehensile Tail, Extra Arm, Disturbing Presence.

Tzeentch: Two Heads, Extra Arm, Very Long Legs.

Khorne: Horns, Iron Hard Skin, Prehensile Tail.

Slaanesh: Tentacles, Foul Appearance, Extra Arm.

Nurgle: Tentacles, Monstrous Mouth, Bone Hook.

Hashut: Iron Hard Skin, Horns, Bone Hook.

Coach's Safe

Deposits: After each game, you may send up to 50k in winnings to a safe for a "rainy day," as well as any number of league winnings.

Restrictions: These funds cannot be spent until the end of the season.

Usage: After a redraft, you may add up to 300k from these reserves when building any team.

Player Advancement (Leveling Up)

Players accumulate SPP and spend them to acquire skills in accordance with the base game rules.

The player's value increases according to the following rules:

Primary skill: +20k

Secondary skill: +40k

AV: +10k

PA: +20k

MA: +30k

AG: +40k

ST: +50k

Elite Combos

Cost Increase: Elite skill combinations increase a player's value by an additional 15k if they were acquired during an advancement.

Elite Skills: The following skills are considered elite:

Claws + Mighty Blow

Guard + Defensive

Wrestle + Evasive

Inducements

Inducements

The following inducements are available in the league:

0-5 Cheerleaders (10k): Receive +1 cheerleader per game.

0-5 Assistant Coaches (10k): Receive +1 assistant coach per game.

0-3 Nuffle's Prayers (15k): Roll on the Nuffle's Prayers table; you may reroll results you already have.

0-1 Team Mascot (25k): Once per half, grants an additional Team Reroll. When used, roll a d6: on a 4+, the reroll works; on a 1-3, it is burned, but you can immediately use a regular reroll instead. Also grants a reroll of 1 for the Cheering Fans event.

0-1 Weather Mage (20k): Once per game, at the start of your turn, you may roll on the weather table, adding a modifier of -2 to +2 to the result after rolling.

0-2 Bloodweiser Keg (or Blitzer's Best) (40k): Until the end of the game, your rolls to recover from Knock Out receive +1.

0-3 Bribe (100k, or 50k for Bribery and Corruption): When your player is sent off, you may spend a bribe to cancel this effect. The turnover is also canceled.

0-6 Additional Training (85k): Gain a Team Reroll until the end of the game.

0-1 Mortuary Assistant (100k, only for Masters of Undeath): Once per game, you may reroll a regeneration check.

0-1 Plague Doctor (100k, only for Favoured of Nurgle): Once per game, you may reroll a regeneration check or apply an apothecary effect.

0-2 Wandering Apothecary (100k, only for teams capable of hiring an Apothecary): Grants an additional use of an apothecary.

0-1 Biased Referee (100k, or 60k for Bribery and Corruption): After your opponent performs a Foul, roll a d6: on a 5+, the fouling player is sent off. You receive +1 to rolls when disputing a referee's decision.

0-1 Hired Wizard (150k): Once per game, after any player's turn, you may cast one of the following spells:

Fireball: Choose a square; roll a d6 for each player in that and adjacent squares. On a 4+, the player falls; make an armor roll with a +1 modifier.

Toad-Toad: Choose a player and roll a d6. If the result equals or exceeds the player's Strength (6 is always a success), turn them into a toad until the end of the drive. Any injury rolls against the toad are considered Badly Hurt; an apothecary cannot be used. Toad stats: MV 5, ST 1, AG 2+, PA -, AV 5+ (Pogo, Evasive, Dodge, Stunty, No Ball, Titchy, Very Long Legs).

0-1 Rowdy Rookies (100k, only for Low cost Linemen): Gain 2d3+1 journeymen, ignoring the team size limit.

0-1 Halfling Master Chef (300k, or 100k for Halfling): At the start of each half, roll a d6 for each of the opponent's Team Rerolls; on a 4+, the reroll transfers to your team.

0-2 Star Players (0-4 for Stunty Leagues): Hire a Star Player from your league, ignoring team limits.

0-1 Vishnevsky Ointment (60k): Once per game, if a player leaves the game due to injury, you may return them to the reserves. However, at the end of the game, apply all effects they should have received from that injury.

0-1 WAAAGH! Drummer (50k, only for Badlands Brawl): At the start of each drive, after kickoff, roll 4d6; for each roll of 4+, move any player 1 square toward your opponent's end zone. You may choose the same player multiple times.

0-3 Frolicking Nurgling (15k, only for Favoured of Nurgle): +1 cheerleader and assistant coach. You may exceed the maximum.

0-1 Rune Priest (50k, only for Old World Classic and Worlds Edge Superleague): At the start of each drive, before the kick, choose one player without the Loner trait and roll a d6: on a 4+, select a rune and apply it to the player (cannot use this effect again for the game); on 2-3, the rune fails but you can repeat in the next drive; on 1, the rune fails and the effect is locked for the game.

Rune of Might: Player gains Mighty Blow until the end of the drive.

Rune of Fury: Player gains Dauntless and Frenzy until the end of the drive.

Rune of Speed: Player gains Sure Feet and Sprint until the end of the drive.

Rune of Iron: Player gains +1 AV and Stand Firm until the end of the drive.

Rune of Force: Player gains Horns and Juggernaut until the end of the drive.

0-1 Ballistics Expert (40k, or 30k for Halfling): Each drive, during kickoff, before the ball scatters, roll a d3:

1: Nothing happens.

2: You may reduce the scatter distance by 1.

3: You may apply a modifier of -2 to +2 to the d6 and d8 values.

0-1 Halfling "Surprise" Pot (80k, or 60k for Halfling): Once per game, after the opponent's turn, choose a square and roll a d6:

6: Pot hits the target.

2-5: Scatter the target square 3 times.

1: d3 players in your reserves move to Knock Out.

The player in the target square falls; make an armor roll with a +2 modifier. Then roll a d6 for each player in adjacent squares; on a 4+, they become Placed Prone.

0-3 Bottles of "Grape Day" (30k, available to Stunty teams): At the start of each drive, before kickoff, choose d3 players with the Stunty rule; until the end of the drive, they gain Dauntless, Frenzy, and Really Stupid (4+).

0-1 Mark of Chaos (50k, only for Favoured of...): At the start of the match, the chosen player gains skills until the end of the game. Does not work on Star Players. Skills depend on your choice of Favoured of...:

Undivided: Tentacles, Horns.

Khorne: Piledriver, Jump Up.

Nurgle: Disturbing Presence, Foul Appearance.

Tzeentch: Pro, Two Heads.

Slaanesh: Dodge, Leap.

Hashut: Iron Hard Skin, Claw.

F.A.Q.

Q: Can a Team Reroll be used more than once per activation? A: Yes.

Q: What happens if a bomb is thrown at a player with the My Ball rule? A: The bomb explodes immediately in the receiving player's square (Commissioner's discretion).

Q: What happens during an inaccurate bomb pass? A: It scatters 3 times and explodes in the final square.

Q: Does a player receive SPP for a knockout if the Thick Skull rule was applied? A: Yes.

Q: A player with AG 1+ dodges out of any number of tackle zones-do they always dodge on a 2+? Since after the roll, the test result is 1, but it's not a natural 1, does that mean the 1+ test is passed? A: No, by analogy with passing, any 1 after modifiers is a failure (Commissioner's discretion).

Q: Can active skills that work at the start of an activation (e.g., Stab, Chainsaw) be used if the player starts the activation in a Prone state? A: Yes. We just want to play like it's 2015, so stand up and stab, foul, etc..

Q: How do Frenzy and Eye Gouge interact with Stand Firm? A: They trigger on any result involving a Push Back, even if the push is ignored.

Q: How does Multiple Block work in conjunction with Stab, Projectile Vomit, and other actions that replace Block? A: Declare Multiple Block before any other skills; both Block actions will be replaced by the special action.

Q: Can Bullseye and Strong Arm be taken by a player with Kick Teammate? A: Yes. Per the rulebook (RAW), this is forbidden, but it appears to be a wording error.

What is Stalling?

If, at the start of your team's turn, a player on your team has the ball and can score without making any dice rolls, then performing any actions other than "reliable" actions will be considered stalling. Additionally, during this turn, if the ball carrier declares any action other than Move or Blitz (provided conditions for reliability are met), it is considered stalling.

Reliable Actions

Movement: This must be done without GFI (Going For It), dodges, or any dice rolls that could potentially lead to a turnover. If the player holding the ball does not score during their activation, it is considered stalling.

Block: Must be a 2 or 3-dice block with a team reroll available, and the active player must have the Block or Wrestle skill. In the event of a "double skull" result, you are obligated to use a team reroll. Intentionally rerolling using Brawler, Hatred, or Pro to force a "double skull" result, as well as choosing a "skull" or "both down" result without applying Block or Wrestle, is considered stalling.

Throw Teammate: Must be performed by a player without the Loner trait, must not involve throwing the ball carrier, and must be performed only when a team reroll is available.

Foul: Must be performed with a bribe available and no modifiers that could negate the bribe

rules.html:
3. Team Management

Team Management

Team Transfer: You may transfer a team from the previous season without any restrictions.

Selling Players: Players can be sold after a match for half their price.

Roster Constraints: You cannot sell or remove a player if doing so would leave the team with fewer than 7 players.

Contracts

Contract Terms: Upon team formation, the coach signs a contract with the team. The contract renewal time is announced by the Commissioner and typically occurs at the end of the season.

Team Buyout: To purchase the team from the league treasury, 600,000 gold will be allocated, plus the funds in the team's treasury at the time of renewal, up to a total maximum of 900,000 gold.

Agent Fees: A player who has completed a contract will incur an additional 20,000 gold in agent fees for each contract completed after the first. For example, after 3 successful contracts, a player will cost 40,000 more to buy out. This increase is not counted toward the total team value.

Player Departure: Players who do not sign a contract leave the team.

Retention: After signing a new contract, a player retains all experience, acquired skills, and injuries.

Dissolution: You may dissolve your team and choose not to renew the contract if you wish.

Contract Renewal

Health Recovery:

Healing Niggling Injuries: Roll a d6 for each Niggling Injury; add a +1 modifier if you have an Apothecary. On a 4+, the vacation and rest work wonders, and the injury is successfully healed. If you fail, the injury remains with the player.

Healing Serious Injuries: Roll a d6 for each stat reduction on the player; add a +1 modifier if you have an Apothecary. On a 4+, the treatment is successful, the characteristic is restored to its previous value, and the player receives a Niggling Injury. If you fail, the injury remains with the player.

Removing Hatred: Roll a d6 for each acquired Hatred skill on the player. On a 4+, the player forgets their old grudges, and the skill is removed from the player's profile.

Staff & Personnel:

Assistant coaches, cheerleaders, and apothecaries go on unpaid leave.

Rerolls are lost.

Select the players you wish to keep on the team and pay their cost, plus any applicable agent fees.

Remove the remaining players. Count the number of upgrades the departing players had and roll that many d6s; for every 1-2 rolled, you lose one Fan Factor.

Purchase new players, staff, and rerolls as needed.

Season Structure and Scoring

Match Conduct:

Rounds: One round lasts two weeks, starting on a Monday and ending on a Sunday.

Deadlines: Each match must be played within its designated round.

Locations: Games can be played at the Litch Club, at Ded Max's Painting Evenings, or at someone's home by prior arrangement.

Virtual Play: If it is impossible to play in real life, the match can be played using the Tabletop Simulator (TTS) mod.

Scheduling: Time proposals for matches must be submitted by the end of Friday. If a coach only provides scheduling proposals over the weekend and the match is ultimately not played, the Commissioner will favor the coach who provided their availability in advance.

Incomplete Matches: If a match is not played within the round, the result will be determined by the Commissioner.

Season Start: At the beginning of the season, all missed game penalties are removed from all players.

League Points:

3 points for a win.

1 point for a draw.

2 points for a technical win.

0 points for a loss.

+1 point if the game ends with a margin of 3+ touchdowns.

+1 point if you conceded 0 touchdowns (you must score at least one yourself).

+2 points if the opponent could not field a team for the match (e.g., due to too many injuries).

End-of-Season Prizes:

At the end of the season, all teams not in the top rankings receive winnings:

10k for each match played.

20k for each victory.

10k for each draw.

Rookie Protection:

For the first 3 games after a team's creation, all injuries inflicted on its players are counted as Badly Hurt.

Technical Win & Tournament Structure

Technical Win: The winning team is awarded 2 MVP rolls and (d3+2) * 10k gold.

Tournament Bracket: Matches are assigned using the Swiss system.

Tie-breakers: The Buchholz coefficient and head-to-head results are used for tie-breakers, followed by the total number of touchdowns and injuries.

Playoff Qualification: Depending on the number of participants, the players at the top of the standings will receive an automatic bye into the tournament's playoff winner's bracket

Additional Rules

Special Rules and Gameplay Changes

Prohibited Inducements: Special Hired Wizards, Biased Referees, and Famous Coaching Staff are not used in the league. DOCX

Rules Adjustments: Some rules from the original Sevens and Blood Bowl have been modified for more enjoyable and deliberate gameplay. Most player costs have been recalculated; these changes can be viewed in the team table. DOCX+ 1

General Gameplay Rules

MVP Selection: Choose 3 players and roll a d3 to decide which one receives 5 SPP. DOCX

Kick-off: When the ball scatters on kick-off, roll 1d6 and halve the result (rounding down); the result can be zero. DOCX

Team Rerolls: The cost of Team Rerolls is not doubled during team drafting, but it is doubled if purchased during the season. DOCX

SPP for Knock Outs: A player who knocks out an opponent receives 1 SPP. DOCX

Experience (SPP):

Actions similar to Block or their consequences grant SPP according to current rules (1 for KO, 2 for Injury). For example, Stab, Chainsaw, Bombardier, and pushing a player off the pitch can now grant experience. DOCX+ 1

A Foul action grants experience just like a Block. DOCX

A Throw Teammate action grants the thrower experience for inflicting Injuries and KOs, just like a Block. DOCX

Inducement Tiering: When determining inducement budgets, the team's tier is considered. Tier 2 teams receive 200k when playing against Tier 1; Tier 3 teams receive 300k against Premier League teams or 100k against Tier 2 teams. DOCX+ 1

Injury Table: Use the injury table from the "big format" (standard) Blood Bowl. DOCX

Setup: The team setup is moved 3 squares forward. Sevens now has a Line of Scrimmage. You must place at least 3 players on the Line of Scrimmage in the central segment. No more than 1 player is allowed in each side segment. DOCX+ 3

Touchdown Restrictions: A touchdown cannot be scored on the first turn of any drive. If your player is in the opponent's end zone at the end of your first turn, you do not score, but if they are still there at the start of your second turn, the drive ends immediately and you score a touchdown. Exception: A touchdown can be scored on the first turn of a drive if the player was thrown via Throw Teammate. DOCX+ 2

Leaping: Jumping over prone players is prohibited unless the player has the Leap skill. DOCX

Mega-Stars: Star Players with the Mega-Star tag occupy both available Star Player slots. DOCX

Bribes: Bribes trigger automatically. DOCX

Secure the Ball!: The "no ball pickup" restriction radius is reduced to 1 square. DOCX

Bribery and Corruption: Allows purchasing up to 3 bribes per team for 50k each, which increase Team Value (TV). DOCX

TV Exceptions: Cheerleaders and assistant coaches do not count toward Team Value. DOCX

Painting Bonus: A fully painted team receives an additional 30k after a match. DOCX

Throw-Ins: Throw-ins are resolved using 1d6.

Team Changes

Necromantic Horror, Shambling Undead, and Tomb Kings: Gained access to hire a Mortuary Assistant (allows a regeneration reroll once per game) for 50k gold.

Nurgle: Gained access to hire a Plague Doctor (allows a regeneration reroll once per game, or acts as an apothecary) for 50k gold.

Team Captain: All teams now have the Team Captain special rule.

Ogre: May designate an Ogre Runt Punter player as captain.

Brawling Brutes: Some teams have gained the Brawling Brutes rule; refer to the team table for details.

Passing Virtuosos: Some teams have gained the new Passing Virtuosos special rule. Teams with this rule receive 2 SPP for touchdowns, and a successful catch during a pass grants the receiver 1 SPP upon completion.

Slann: Now a Tier 1 team. All Slann players replace the Pogo skill with Leap.

Halfling: Gained access to the Old World Classic league.

Goblin: Pogoer, Ooligan, and Doom Diver players now have the Lineman tag.

Chaos Chosen: Chaos Blocker players now have Iron Hard Skin.

Norse: The Beer Boar player now has the Lineman tag; the Norse Raider Lineman no longer has the Unsteady rule.

High Elf: Updated according to current rules, and costs have been revised.

Elven Union: Elf Lineman players have lost the Fumblerooskie skill.

Gnome: The team list has been completely reworked.

Orc: The Big Un no longer has the Mighty Blow skill.

Black Orc: Black Orc movement (MV) increased to 5, and their cost is now 110k. The team has been moved to Tier 1.

Chaos Dwarf: The team has been reworked.

Dwarf: Dwarf Blocker Lineman now have 'S' as primary access and 'D' as secondary access. The Deathroller now costs 180k, and the team has been moved to Tier 1.

Khorne: The Bloodborn Marauder Lineman has had their PA reduced to 6+, AV increased to 9+, gained the Brawler skill, and now costs 60k. The team has been moved to Tier 1.

Tomb Kings: Ancient Skeleton Lineman players now have primary access to 'D'. Blitz-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 65k. Thro-Ra AG increased to 3+, price is now 85k. The team has been moved to Tier 1, and the Brawling Brutes tag has been removed.

Kislev: The team has been reworked.

Vampire: Thrall Lineman now cost 40k. Vampire Thrower now cost 115k. It is now forbidden to hire an apothecary for the team.

:

Skill Changes

Claws, Mighty Blow, and Piledriver: These skills now fully stack (with Claws and Mighty Blow, you break any armor on a 7+).

Piledriver: Completely changed. After an armor roll from a Block action, the player using the skill can become Placed Prone to roll again. This does not work with skills that replace Block and has been moved to the Strength category.

Hypnotic Gaze: Partially changed; can now only be used once per turn.

Brawler: Completely changed; allows for a one-time reroll of a single die during a Block. Cannot be used in negative dice blocks.

Plague Ridden/Masters of Undeath: Completely changed; when a player with this rule kills an opponent, their coach gains a Lineman of their own team (Shambling Undead can choose between a Skeleton or a Zombie).

Throw Teammate: Now a passive skill.

Blood Lust: New wording. Roll a d6 after declaring any action: on a 2+, the turn continues normally. On a 1, the player must "feed." The action is lost; the player may choose an adjacent teammate (without the Vampire or Undead tags) to perform an injury roll against, treating all results as Badly Hurt. This roll causes a turnover only if the Thrall was holding the ball. Movement may be declared before the bite; after the bite, the player's activation ends. If no bite is performed, a turnover occurs.

Accurate and Cannoneer: Merged into one skill (Accurate), which provides +1 to all passes.

Dodge: Completely changed; now only allows a reroll for dodge tests.

Evasive (New): The player can treat a result of 5 as 3-4 when being blocked, and Stumble results as Push Back.

Tackle: Completely changed; it now ignores the Evasive skill.

Arm Bar: Completely changed; prevents rerolls on dodge tests for players in the owner's tackle zone. Grants SPP like Block if the opponent is injured or knocked out while dodging away.

Block: Partially changed; only works during the player's own activation.

Thick Skull: Moved to the General category.

Sneaky Git: You are no longer removed on doubles when fouling during an armor break.

Stab: No longer ends movement during a Blitz.

Shiv (New): Works like Stab but can only be used once per turn among all players with the skill.

Decay: Completely changed; when receiving an injury, roll two dice and choose the highest.

Safe Pass: Protects against any Fumble, not just on a natural 1.

Fumblerooski: Moved to the Agility category.

Pogo: Can only be used once per activation.

Secret Weapon: Only works if the player was fielded for the drive.

Shadowing: Now a General skill.

Frenzy: Now optional; must be declared immediately before the first block action of the activation.

Monstrous Mouth: Limits holding to 1 player (unless used with Multiple Block, then up to 2).

Big Hand: Additionally allows picking up the ball from an adjacent square (but not while in an opponent's control zone).

Disturbing Presence: Works on ball pickup (still does not affect Secure the Ball); does not work if the player is prone.

Foul Appearance: Now triggers on a 1-2 roll and does not end the player's activation.

Iron Hard Skin: Now grants +1 AV in addition to basic properties; the modifier ignore only works during a block.

Tentacles: Now works on a raw 4+ roll, regardless of the models' strength.

Bone Hook (New Mutation): Before a Foul action, you may move the target player 1 square to an adjacent cell (happens before assist calculation).

Leap: Now always works on an unmodifiable 4+ roll (improved by the Very Long Legs mutation).

Elvenball (New General Skill): Grants the ability to move after a Secure the Ball action.

Guard: Now only works during the opponent's turn.

Offensive (New General Skill): Grants an unblockable assist for a block during your turn.

Defensive: Now cancels the Offensive skill.

Breathe Fire: Does not cause Knocked Down on a roll of 1.

Diving Catch / On the Ball: Work when Punt is declared.

Jump Up: Allows replacing Block with special actions (e.g., Stab) and declaring active skills while Prone.

Multiple Block: Works on special actions that replace Block.

Juggernaut: Also cancels Foul Appearance.

Kick: Changed; instead of a d3 roll, halve the d6 value during kickoff (rounding down, result can be 0).

Leader: Triggers at the start of a drive.

Changes to "Favoured Of..."

Real Bonuses: The Favoured of rule now provides actual bonuses.

Deity Selection: If a team has a choice for this rule, you must select one of the following options during team registration: Undivided, Khorne, Nurgle, Tzeentch, Slaanesh, or Hashut.

Skill Acquisition: Every time a player receives an advancement, roll a d8.

Undivided triggers on 1.

Slaanesh triggers on 6.

Nurgle triggers on 7.

Khorne triggers on 8.

Tzeentch triggers on 9 (or 6).

Hashut triggers on 4.

Additional Skill: A player may receive one of the skills corresponding to your choice in addition to the one taken for a regular advancement. This skill increases the player's value, though it does not increase the player's rank.

Random Selection: If a player has 'M' access in secondary skills or no access at all, they must choose the skill randomly.

Skill Lists:

Undivided: Prehensile Tail, Extra Arm, Disturbing Presence.

Tzeentch: Two Heads, Extra Arm, Very Long Legs.

Khorne: Horns, Iron Hard Skin, Prehensile Tail.

Slaanesh: Tentacles, Foul Appearance, Extra Arm.

Nurgle: Tentacles, Monstrous Mouth, Bone Hook.

Hashut: Iron Hard Skin, Horns, Bone Hook.

Coach's Safe

Deposits: After each game, you may send up to 50k in winnings to a safe for a "rainy day," as well as any number of league winnings.

Restrictions: These funds cannot be spent until the end of the season.

Usage: After a redraft, you may add up to 300k from these reserves when building any team.

Player Advancement (Leveling Up)

Players accumulate SPP and spend them to acquire skills in accordance with the base game rules.

The player's value increases according to the following rules:

Primary skill: +20k

Secondary skill: +40k

AV: +10k

PA: +20k

MA: +30k

AG: +40k

ST: +50k

Elite Combos

Cost Increase: Elite skill combinations increase a player's value by an additional 15k if they were acquired during an advancement.

Elite Skills: The following skills are considered elite:

Claws + Mighty Blow

Guard + Defensive

Wrestle + Evasive

Evasive

Thick Skull

Bone Hook

13

Fumblerooski

Shadowing

14

Offensive

15

Elvenball

Inducements

Inducements

The following inducements are available in the league:

0-5 Cheerleaders (10k): Receive +1 cheerleader per game.

0-5 Assistant Coaches (10k): Receive +1 assistant coach per game.

0-3 Nuffle's Prayers (15k): Roll on the Nuffle's Prayers table; you may reroll results you already have.

0-1 Team Mascot (25k): Once per half, grants an additional Team Reroll. When used, roll a d6: on a 4+, the reroll works; on a 1-3, it is burned, but you can immediately use a regular reroll instead. Also grants a reroll of 1 for the Cheering Fans event.

0-1 Weather Mage (20k): Once per game, at the start of your turn, you may roll on the weather table, adding a modifier of -2 to +2 to the result after rolling.

0-2 Bloodweiser Keg (or Blitzer's Best) (40k): Until the end of the game, your rolls to recover from Knock Out receive +1.

0-3 Bribe (100k, or 50k for Bribery and Corruption): When your player is sent off, you may spend a bribe to cancel this effect. The turnover is also canceled.

0-6 Additional Training (85k): Gain a Team Reroll until the end of the game.

0-1 Mortuary Assistant (100k, only for Masters of Undeath): Once per game, you may reroll a regeneration check.

0-1 Plague Doctor (100k, only for Favoured of Nurgle): Once per game, you may reroll a regeneration check or apply an apothecary effect.

0-2 Wandering Apothecary (100k, only for teams capable of hiring an Apothecary): Grants an additional use of an apothecary.

0-1 Biased Referee (100k, or 60k for Bribery and Corruption): After your opponent performs a Foul, roll a d6: on a 5+, the fouling player is sent off. You receive +1 to rolls when disputing a referee's decision.

0-1 Hired Wizard (150k): Once per game, after any player's turn, you may cast one of the following spells:

Fireball: Choose a square; roll a d6 for each player in that and adjacent squares. On a 4+, the player falls; make an armor roll with a +1 modifier.

Toad-Toad: Choose a player and roll a d6. If the result equals or exceeds the player's Strength (6 is always a success), turn them into a toad until the end of the drive. Any injury rolls against the toad are considered Badly Hurt; an apothecary cannot be used. Toad stats: MV 5, ST 1, AG 2+, PA -, AV 5+ (Pogo, Evasive, Dodge, Stunty, No Ball, Titchy, Very Long Legs).

0-1 Rowdy Rookies (100k, only for Low cost Linemen): Gain 2d3+1 journeymen, ignoring the team size limit.

0-1 Halfling Master Chef (300k, or 100k for Halfling): At the start of each half, roll a d6 for each of the opponent's Team Rerolls; on a 4+, the reroll transfers to your team.

0-2 Star Players (0-4 for Stunty Leagues): Hire a Star Player from your league, ignoring team limits.

0-1 Vishnevsky Ointment (60k): Once per game, if a player leaves the game due to injury, you may return them to the reserves. However, at the end of the game, apply all effects they should have received from that injury.

0-1 WAAAGH! Drummer (50k, only for Badlands Brawl): At the start of each drive, after kickoff, roll 4d6; for each roll of 4+, move any player 1 square toward your opponent's end zone. You may choose the same player multiple times.

0-3 Frolicking Nurgling (15k, only for Favoured of Nurgle): +1 cheerleader and assistant coach. You may exceed the maximum.

0-1 Rune Priest (50k, only for Old World Classic and Worlds Edge Superleague): At the start of each drive, before the kick, choose one player without the Loner trait and roll a d6: on a 4+, select a rune and apply it to the player (cannot use this effect again for the game); on 2-3, the rune fails but you can repeat in the next drive; on 1, the rune fails and the effect is locked for the game.

Rune of Might: Player gains Mighty Blow until the end of the drive.

Rune of Fury: Player gains Dauntless and Frenzy until the end of the drive.

Rune of Speed: Player gains Sure Feet and Sprint until the end of the drive.

Rune of Iron: Player gains +1 AV and Stand Firm until the end of the drive.

Rune of Force: Player gains Horns and Juggernaut until the end of the drive.

0-1 Ballistics Expert (40k, or 30k for Halfling): Each drive, during kickoff, before the ball scatters, roll a d3:

1: Nothing happens.

2: You may reduce the scatter distance by 1.

3: You may apply a modifier of -2 to +2 to the d6 and d8 values.

0-1 Halfling "Surprise" Pot (80k, or 60k for Halfling): Once per game, after the opponent's turn, choose a square and roll a d6:

6: Pot hits the target.

2-5: Scatter the target square 3 times.

1: d3 players in your reserves move to Knock Out.

The player in the target square falls; make an armor roll with a +2 modifier. Then roll a d6 for each player in adjacent squares; on a 4+, they become Placed Prone.

0-3 Bottles of "Grape Day" (30k, available to Stunty teams): At the start of each drive, before kickoff, choose d3 players with the Stunty rule; until the end of the drive, they gain Dauntless, Frenzy, and Really Stupid (4+).

0-1 Mark of Chaos (50k, only for Favoured of...): At the start of the match, the chosen player gains skills until the end of the game. Does not work on Star Players. Skills depend on your choice of Favoured of...:

Undivided: Tentacles, Horns.

Khorne: Piledriver, Jump Up.

Nurgle: Disturbing Presence, Foul Appearance.

Tzeentch: Pro, Two Heads.

Slaanesh: Dodge, Leap.

Hashut: Iron Hard Skin, Claw.

F.A.Q.

Q: Can a Team Reroll be used more than once per activation? A: Yes.

Q: What happens if a bomb is thrown at a player with the My Ball rule? A: The bomb explodes immediately in the receiving player's square (Commissioner's discretion).

Q: What happens during an inaccurate bomb pass? A: It scatters 3 times and explodes in the final square.

Q: Does a player receive SPP for a knockout if the Thick Skull rule was applied? A: Yes.

Q: A player with AG 1+ dodges out of any number of tackle zones-do they always dodge on a 2+? Since after the roll, the test result is 1, but it's not a natural 1, does that mean the 1+ test is passed? A: No, by analogy with passing, any 1 after modifiers is a failure (Commissioner's discretion).

Q: Can active skills that work at the start of an activation (e.g., Stab, Chainsaw) be used if the player starts the activation in a Prone state? A: Yes. We just want to play like it's 2015, so stand up and stab, foul, etc..

Q: How do Frenzy and Eye Gouge interact with Stand Firm? A: They trigger on any result involving a Push Back, even if the push is ignored.

Q: How does Multiple Block work in conjunction with Stab, Projectile Vomit, and other actions that replace Block? A: Declare Multiple Block before any other skills; both Block actions will be replaced by the special action.

Q: Can Bullseye and Strong Arm be taken by a player with Kick Teammate? A: Yes. Per the rulebook (RAW), this is forbidden, but it appears to be a wording error.

What is Stalling?

If, at the start of your team's turn, a player on your team has the ball and can score without making any dice rolls, then performing any actions other than "reliable" actions will be considered stalling. Additionally, during this turn, if the ball carrier declares any action other than Move or Blitz (provided conditions for reliability are met), it is considered stalling.

Reliable Actions

Movement: This must be done without GFI (Going For It), dodges, or any dice rolls that could potentially lead to a turnover. If the player holding the ball does not score during their activation, it is considered stalling.

Block: Must be a 2 or 3-dice block with a team reroll available, and the active player must have the Block or Wrestle skill. In the event of a "double skull" result, you are obligated to use a team reroll. Intentionally rerolling using Brawler, Hatred, or Pro to force a "double skull" result, as well as choosing a "skull" or "both down" result without applying Block or Wrestle, is considered stalling.

Throw Teammate: Must be performed by a player without the Loner trait, must not involve throwing the ball carrier, and must be performed only when a team reroll is available.

Foul: Must be performed with a bribe available and no modifiers that could negate the bribe
